entry.title: Choosing Between SAP Extended Warehouse Management and SAP Logistics Management
entry.content:
SAP's warehouse management portfolio now offers three distinct paths: SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud, SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Public Cloud, and the newer warehouse management solution released officially for customers in March 2026: SAP Logistics Management

In this blog, I would like to help customers select the best path between SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud and SAP Logistics Management, based on their intralogistics profile.

What Is SAP Logistics Management?

Before comparing the solutions, let me address the most frequent question I hear from customers: "What is SAP Logistics Management?"

In a nutshell, SAP Logistics Management is a cloud-based logistics solution that combines warehouse management, transportation management capabilities, and Business Network for Logistics to optimize operations through network-centric collaboration. As a new SaaS offering, it unites warehousing and transportation dispatching, helping businesses optimize their logistics fulfillment operations and improve overall efficiency.

Setting the Foundation

This high-level definition serves as an initial foundation for any customer planning to implement an SAP warehouse management solution. The natural next question within the organization is: Which solution best fits my operations?

Both solutions serve distinct warehouse business models, and this statement should guide you on solution evaluation and due to this I would like to provide clarity and help customers avoid common mistakes when deciding between SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud (Embedded or Decentralized) and SAP Logistics Management.

Core Principle

Before starting any discussion, it is essential to understand one key point:

SAP Logistics Management does not replace SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud.

Instead, SAP Logistics Management extends SAP's warehouse portfolio, enabling organizations to optimize every site—not just large and complex distribution centers.

These two solutions serve different intralogistics profiles, with a deliberate overlap zone where either option may be appropriate depending on specific business needs.

The diagram below shows, at a high level, which solution is recommended based on the intralogistics profile. In the overlap zone (highlighted in yellow), both solutions might be applicable. Let us take a deeper look at the different features and scenarios that can guide you toward the right decision

IMPORTANT: Unlike SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud—where new features or enhancements are released with each Feature Package Stack (FPS) or new SAP S/4HANA version—SAP Logistics Management delivers new features and enhancements on a bi-weekly basis. Visit  What’s New Viewer regularly to stay up to date with the latest content.

When to Choose SAP Logistics Management

SAP Logistics Management is best suited for organizations with low-to-mid complexity warehouses. It is designed for smaller warehousing and shipping facilities, scaling businesses, and second-tier operations of large enterprises. It seamlessly integrates with your core SAP S/4HANA Cloud (ERP) order processing and your network of logistics service providers and carriers.

Typical Characteristics

  • Full transparency into logistics operations
  • Scalable growth
  • Ease of access to carrier networks
  • Low-to-medium operational complexity
  • Need for rapid deployment across multiple sites
  • Focus on guided execution rather than deep optimization
  • Desire to eliminate paper-based processes and digitize operations
  • Limited or no advanced automation or robotics
  • Strong emphasis on speed of implementation and lower TCO

Common Warehouse Types

  • Local or regional replenishment centers
  • E-commerce micro-fulfillment centers
  • Production-support warehouses
  • Service and MRO warehouses for internal consumption

When to Choose SAP Extended Warehouse Management

SAP Extended Warehouse Management is designed for highly complex, high-volume warehouse operations requiring advanced optimization and automation.

Typical Characteristics

  • High throughput and performance requirements
  • Peak season processing with large volume spikes
  • Very high task volumes (thousands per hour)
  • Advanced wave management with multiple warehouse requests
  • Intensive use of transport units
  • Integration with warehouse automation technologies

Supported Automation

  • Conveyor systems
  • AS/RS (Automated Storage and Retrieval Systems)
  • AGVs (Automated Guided Vehicles)
  • AMRs (Autonomous Mobile Robots)
  • Material Flow System (MFS)
  • Voice-directed picking

Advanced Capabilities

  • Complex process models (POSC/LOSC)
  • Cross-docking and merchandise flow-through
  • Yard management integration
  • Slotting and rearrangement
  • Task interleaving
  • Labor management and demand planning

The Overlap Zone: When Both Could Fit

Some warehouses fall into a gray area where either SAP Logistics Management or SAP Extended Warehouse Management could be appropriate. In these cases, a deeper evaluation is required.

Choose SAP Logistics Management if:

  • You want a unified platform combining Transportation, Warehouse Management, and Business Network
  • You prefer a modular, low-complexity solution
  • You need a simple execution layer
  • Automation is limited or moderate
  • You prioritize cloud-native architecture
  • You aim for reduced total cost of ownership (TCO)
  • You require a global template with fast rollout capabilities

Choose SAP Extended Warehouse Management if:

  • Your strategy is evolving toward advanced automation
  • You operate in a complex IT landscape integrating multiple ERP systems
  • Advanced routing and picking optimization are must-have capabilities
  • Your warehouse is expected to scale into a high-throughput environment
  • You handle complex exception scenarios
  • You plan to leverage Labor Management, advanced interleaving, or AI-driven optimization

Conclusion

The purpose is not to frame the decision as "old vs. new," but rather as "best fit for each site."

Organizations with diverse warehouse networks may benefit from using both solutions:

  • SAP Extended Warehouse Management for Private Cloud for high-throughput and complex distribution centers
  • SAP Logistics Management for smaller, less complex sites that still require structured, digital execution

Ultimately, the key question is not: Which solution is better? But rather: Where is each warehouse today—and where is it going?

entry.title: AI-Assisted Journal Upload
entry.content:
I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I am coming back to this community with an SAP strategic topic: Business AI.

            More specifically, Autonomous Finance. AI is the cornerstone of SAP Autonomous Enterprise offering, that revolutionize the way we support our customers businesses: Innovations that develop state-of the-art tools and agents to automate tasks, where humans can be released of cumbersome tasks to focus on business intelligence and financial engineering to maximize benefits with real-time value.           

            As part of the SAP Autonomous Enterprise, SAP Autonomous Finance is built out of Joule Assistants that orchestrate all the Finance agents that support this business process. AI-assisted Journal Upload is part of Financial Closing Assistant, that drives the full close lifecycle across posting, accruals, journal validation, reconciliations, and so on. The major goal of Financial Closing Assistant is to reducing closing time and streamlines the reporting associated to closing.

Figure 1: SAP Finance Assistants-Financial Closing Assistant

            For years, SAP has developed solutions that enable customers to perform specific business tasks which, while executed outside the ERP system and often requiring significant time and effort, provide critical input for gaining a complete view of the business. Tasks that, if not performed, can disrupt business operations and compromise data visibility. Uploading journal entries is one of them. It is either a periodic or frequent activity or both, in every landscape: satellite systems, legacies, integrations that support the operation in a way or another, that feedback the ERP with transactions that must be posted to keep the accounting books updated.

            Many of us have used the different ways to upload journal entries to the ERP: The traditional file (we used to call it "flat") with a fixed structure of columns with specific format. Another is the journal entry template, for postings based on your needs, where fields and values can be selected to produce a tailored-specific template for postings, taking out non-relevant fields and focusing only on the desired ones. This template can also be shared and with pre-defined level of use (group, company, user). Please look at Fiori app Manage Journal Entry Templates (F3803)documentation to know of this, in case you are interested. Both have been extremely useful tools, widely used by customers and partners.

            Month-end journal entries upload is more needed than ever. Increasingly complex system landscapes, new integration points, constantly evolving business models, and the ever-changing business environment demonstrate that, if this process can be executed autonomously with minimal supervision, the resulting efficiency gains and cost savings can make a significant contribution to the organization's value creation efforts. 

What is AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

            As period-end journal entries are necessary for period closing, accounting teams are spending considerable time in curating data as per the policies and reporting needs. Through new SAP Fiori apps with Generative AI capabilities embedded, AI-assisted Journal Upload in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition accelerates the creation and the upload of period-end manual journal entries.

            AI-assisted Journal Upload is a Financial AI feature to speed-up the creation and the upload of period-end manual journal entries.

            With AI-assisted Journal Upload, accountants can:

  • Assign a guidance document (written in natural language) for posting to the journal upload case
  • Create journal upload cases
  • Generate journal posting proposals according to the assigned guidance
  • Validate the posting proposals before posting and
  • Post the posting proposal as journals

What are typical business cases for AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

            Business scenarios best suited for AI-assisted Journal Upload include recurring general ledger journal entries with a high volume of records and similar information requirements, making them ideal candidates for this autonomous feature. Other excellent use cases include large volumes of prepaid expenses, lease or rental in advance-payments, and customer prepayments, all of which can be efficiently processed using AI-assisted Journal Upload.

Is AI-assisted Journal Upload intended for large companies only?

            Not at all. Regardless of their size, Companies often pay for services they will receive in the future. Small and large businesses work with prepaid expenses frequently. It is a way to secure better terms, benefit from early-payment discounts, and take advantage of periods of stronger cash flow while ensuring futre obligations are covered.

            When the volume of prepaid operations is high, as they happen in advance, a proper tracking is crucial for financial control and compliance, to record them when is right to do it (in the posting period when each service is received). Plus, a posting template easy to use and populate to post to the right accounting objects, is invaluable for saving time and reducing the risk of errors during the financial closing. The easiest, the better. These are some of the advantages of using AI-assisted Journal Upload.

            In the graphic below, we find some interesting figures about uploading journal entries for a business (complexity varies in each case). It can be found on SAP Discovery Center - S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - AI-Assisted Journal Upload:

Figure 2: AI-assisted Journal Upload - Estimated benefits

            Technically speaking, AI-Assisted Journal Upload is an embedded Intelligent Scenario delivered via SAP's Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management (ISLM) framework, using scenario FIN_INTLGNT_JRNLUPLD. It is natively embedded within SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, with the underlying AI service provisioned through SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) as a backend infrastructure integration — not a side-by-side deployment.

What are the specifications of AI-Assisted Journal Upload?

  • Identifier: J247
  • AI Type: AI Feature
  • Support Component: FI-GL-AI-IJU
  • Minimun Required System Version: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS03
  • Required Roles & Authorizations:
    • BTP: Global Account Administrator role, assigned via Security > Role Collections in the BTP Cockpit
    • S/4HANA: ISLM Administrator role to manage intelligent scenario activation
  • BTP Subaccount & Entitlement Requirements:
    • A valid BTP subaccount must exist and be accessible
    • The AI service entitlement must be available and assigned to the relevant subaccount prior to service instance creation

What do we need to start using AI-assisted Journal Upload?

            3 easy steps to determine AI-assisted Journal Upload readiness:

  • Check 1 - To verify the Scenario Status in Intelligent Scenario Management (F4470)  Fiori app, you need to confirm that FIN_INTLGNT_JRNLUPLD shows a Published status with no errors in the ISLM app
 
 

 


 

Figure 3: Intelligent Scenario Management

 
 

 


 

Figure 4: Manage Journal Posting Policies
Check 2 - Confirm that Fiori App Manage Journal Posting Policies (F8539) is available in Fiori Launchpad (FLP). Check role authorizations and catalogs if it is not visible

 
 

 


 

Figure 5: Manage Journal Upload Cases
Check 3 - Confirm that Fiori App Manage Journal Upload Cases (F8538) is available in Fiori Launchpad (FLP). Same considerations as F8539 here.

            Also, it is always good to make sure that content in SAP Note 3573144 AI-assisted Journal Upload in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private has been implemented in the system.

            Let me share the link to my blog post AI-assisted Journal Upload - How to use it .

            I hope you find this insightful. In my next blog, I will go step by step on how to use AI-assisted Journal Upload.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team

entry.title: AI-Assisted Journal Upload - How to use it
entry.content:
I am Betty Guedez, Product & Engineering Solution Architect at SAP Regional Implementation Group RIG. I am coming back with the second part of my blog post AI-assisted Journal Upload . This time, how to use it.

            Remember that AI-Assisted Journal Upload is an AI feature, part of the SAP Autonomous Finance offering.

            We also know that SAP Autonomous Finance is one of the Joule Assistants in SAP Autonomous Enterprise platform, and it is there to that orchestrate all the Finance agents that support this business process. AI-assisted Journal Upload is part of Financial Closing Assistant, that drives the full close lifecycle across posting, accruals, journal validation, reconciliations, and so on.           

Start using AI-assisted Journal Upload

            AI-assisted Journal Upload requires a couple of IMG-customizing-related activities in the backend, related to the category and subcategory of the journal entries you will post. Following the path Financial Accounting => Financial Accounting Global Settings => Document => Intelligent Journal Uploads, you will see the two activities, Define Journal Upload Case Categories and Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories. Together, they both serve to give a classification to the expenses that better explain your postings.

Define Journal Upload Case Categories:

Figure 1: Define Journal Upload Case Categories

 Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories:

Figure 2: Define Journal Upload Case Subcategories


          Next, to download and adapt the policy template, we access the Fiori App Manage Journal Posting Policies (F8539). With this app, we set up all the relevant fields and the natural language instructions to populate the fields on the template.

 
 

 


 

Figure 3: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Initial Screen


          Click on Download Template, select the format (Spreadsheet, PDF) and OK. (I feel more comfortable with the excel file, so I chose the first one).

 
 

 


 

Figure 4: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Template Downloaded

            Follow the guidelines provided and update the Input instructions that the feature  is going to use to handle the data in the input file. It is important to bear in mind:

  • For now, Cardinality - Optional/Mandatory (O/M)- field is not required. It will be supported in future releases, so you may leave it blank.
  • The file name format must be "Name", followed by a "_", followed by the "Language Code".ext (xslx, pdf). Supported language codes are EN (English), DE (German), ES (Spanish), FR (French), PT (Portuguese), JA (Japanese), ZH (Simplified Chinese). Example: "Policy Document 2026_EN.xlsx".
  • Make sure that the fields referenced in the Input Instruction of a field in the policy document, are columns in the input data file, for example:
 
 

 


 

Figure 5: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Template - Input Instruction examples

            Based on the instruction for field WRBTR, there should be a column labeled "Amount" with values in the input file.                  And now, let's work with the policy in the system:

Figure 6: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Policy Creation

Case category and Subcategory: According to customizing mentioned above

Description: Short Text about the policy purpose (max 50 characters)

Valid to: Defaulted to 12/31/9999

Valid from: Starting validity date of the policy. If there is an existing policy of the same Category and Subcategory, with Valid from date prior or equal to this, the system will raise an error, and the policy cannot be saved. The policy must start after the starting date of any previously created policy of this service.

 
 

 


 

Figure 7: Manage Journal Posting Policies - Policy Activation

            Once created, the policy needs to be Activated.

            Now, it is the time to upload the input data to post the journal entries.

            We designed a simplified excel file that contains the data of the charges received for shuttle services for 3 employees, to create the postings for the expenses charged for this service:

Figure 8: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Sample input data


       To do that, open the Fiori app Manage Journal Upload Cases (F8538) and hit Create:

Figure 9: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Updating the Posting Proposal

            Once created, a Proposal must be generated:

Figure 10: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Generating the Posting Proposal

            Proposal Validation:

Figure 11: Manage Journal Upload Cases: Validating the Posting Proposal

            And the proposal finally to be posted:

Figure 12: Manage Upload Cases: Posting the Proposal

            Let's display the postings in the Fiori app Manage Journal Entries (F0717A):

Figure 13: Manage Journal Entries: Displaying the Postings

            The posted document SA 1000000006 shows that rides of the three employees using the shuttle bus service in the second week of June 2026 were posted to the right posting period (06/2026), crediting the prepaid expense (which is currently a right, in consequence) for $264,00 USD.

            AI-assisted Journal Upload is a key component of the SAP Autonomous Enterprise offering and provides an easy, straightforward process to generate postings in S/4HANA from external sources, as part of the month-end closing, releasing the business experts from spending lots of time and effort fulfilling templates that can be well known, but still somewhat error-prone.

            Before closing, let me share two more useful resources with you.

SAP AI Services in Discovery Center

AI-assisted Journal Upload in help.sap.com

            I hope you find this insightful.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team

entry.title: SAP Green Ledger: Q2-26 Release Updates and Highlights
entry.content:
Welcome to the Q2 2026 edition of our SAP Green Ledger Quarterly Release Highlights! This blog summarizes the most important SAP Green Ledger innovations and enhancements delivered during Q2 2026. Highlights include new capabilities for carbon traceability, carbon collection processing, Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM), carbon allocations, analytics, and usability improvements across SAP Green Ledger.

Read on to learn more about the key innovations introduced throughout Q2 2026:

  • Carbon Traceability with Related SAP S/4HANA Documents
  • Mass Reversal of Carbon Collections
  • Simplified CBAM Processing
  • Flexible Carbon Allocations
  • Analytical Dashboard Update
  • Further Updates and Enhancements

 

Carbon Traceability with Related SAP S/4HANA Documents

The Manage Carbon Collections app now includes a new "Related Documents" section that allows you to maintain and view references to the SAP S/4HANA documents from which emissions originate. Supported reference types include:

  • Purchasing Documents
  • Accounting Documents
  • Other Documents

If you import material movements into SAP Green Ledger from a connected SAP S/4HANA system, related document references are transferred automatically. When importing carbon data via CSV files, you can also include related document information in the file. For manually created carbon collections, you can maintain related documents directly in the Manage Carbon Collections app. These references are then carried through from the carbon collection into the corresponding journal entries in the Manage Journal Entries app. This allows you to trace carbon postings throughout the accounting process, from the originating business documents to the resulting carbon journal entries, also improving auditability of carbon data.

Related Documents in a Carbon CollectionTo further simplify navigation and reporting, you can now also filter in the Manage Carbon Collections, Manage Journal Entries and Display Line Items apps by Related Document Type and Related Document ID. This helps you quickly locate carbon records for specific business transactions. For more details check SAP Help: Create a Carbon Collection and Use a CSV Template.

 

Mass Reversal of Carbon Collections

The Manage Carbon Collections app now supports reversing multiple posted carbon collections in a single step. Instead of reversing each carbon collection individually, you can now select and reverse multiple posted carbon collections at once. This significantly reduces manual effort, particularly after correcting mass uploads. Up to 5,000 carbon collections can be reversed in a single operation. For more information, see SAP Help: Post a Carbon Collection to the Journal.

 

Simplified CBAM Processing

Several enhancements improve the processing and monitoring of Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism (CBAM) carbon collections while also increasing transparency for CBAM accounts configuration.

Efficient Processing of CBAM Carbon Collections

The Manage Carbon Collections app now also supports reversing CBAM carbon collections. In addition, you can now post and reverse multiple CBAM carbon collections in a single step. As a result, you can process large volumes of carbon collections that require carbon certificates more efficiently.

When reversing CBAM carbon collections, the resulting journal entries are created with the journal entry source type Carbon Collection Reversal, providing a clear audit trail for correction postings. When posting or reversing multiple CBAM carbon collections, SAP Green Ledger automatically calculates a single fair value per company code. This reduces manual effort and ensures consistent valuation across company codes.

To support the monitoring of these postings, the Manage Carbon Liabilities app now supports filtering carbon liabilities by Posted and Reversed status. With that, you can quickly monitor the processing status of carbon liabilities and identify relevant entries. Check out SAP Help for more details: Post a Carbon Collection to the Journal, Fair Value Calculation, and View Carbon Liabilities.

Filter for Posted and Reversed Carbon Liabilities in the Manage Carbon Liabilities App

Improved Visibility into EU CBAM Accounts by Company Code

The Configure Carbon Certificate Data app now displays the EU CBAM accounts maintained in the Determine Mandatory Accounts app for each company code. This allows you to view the configured account assignments directly while working with carbon certificate data, without switching between applications. See also SAP Help: Configure Carbon Certificate Data and Determine Mandatory Accounts.

 EU CBAM Accounts per Company Code in Configure Carbon Certificate Data App

 

Flexible Carbon Allocations

Several enhancements make carbon allocations more flexible and align them more closely with financial accounting. Our new capabilities can help you reuse existing allocation logic and support project-based allocation scenarios.

Copy Financial Flow for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The ability to create allocation models by copying financial flows in the Manage Allocations app is now also available for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Previously available only for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, this enhancement enables you to create carbon allocation models based on the allocation patterns already used in your connected SAP S/4HANA system.

Allocation Model Copied from Financial Flow

Using the latest Cost Center Accounting data from the connected SAP S/4HANA system, SAP Green Ledger automatically copies the receiving objects and allocation percentages and transfers them into the allocation model. This eliminates the need to manually maintain allocation rules and helps keep carbon allocations aligned with financial allocations. For more details, check SAP Help: Create Allocation Models by Copying from Financial Flow.

Project-Level Carbon Allocations with WBS Elements

The Manage Allocations app now supports Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) elements as both sender and receiver dimensions when creating periodic allocation models. For both the sender and receiver, you can now choose a WBS element or a cost center as the allocation dimension, enabling allocation scenarios such as WBS-to-WBS, WBS-to-Cost Center, Cost Center-to-WBS, and Cost Center-to-Cost Center. To ensure consistency with the existing allocation behavior for cost centers, the corresponding Profit Center and Segment are now automatically derived as well when a WBS element is used as the receiver dimension.

WBS Elements Used as Sender and Receiver Dimensions in a Periodic Allocation Model

 To further improve transparency, Partner WBS Elements are now included in journal entry line items and displayed in both the Manage Journal Entries and Display Line Items apps. You can also filter, group, and sort by Partner WBS Element in the Display Line Items app, giving you better visibility into carbon allocation flows across project structures. For more details, see Create Period Allocation Models.

 

Analytical Dashboard Update

The SAP Green Ledger Analytical Dashboard has also been updated for Q2 2026 with the latest analytical capabilities for combined financial and carbon reporting. For a detailed overview of these enhancements, see the dedicated SAP Community blog post and the SAP Help Portal topic Analyze Carbon Data Alongside Financial Data.

 

Further Updates and Enhancements

Several smaller enhancements further improve the usability of SAP Green Ledger.

Improved Journal Entry Usability

The Manage Journal Entries app now allows you to maximize the Line Items table. You can review journal entries with many line items more comfortably and reduce the need for scrolling. Additionally, each line item is now assigned a stable unique identifier that remains unchanged when the table is sorted or filtered. The stable identifier makes it easier to reference individual line items and improves navigation during journal analysis.

Updated Posting Terminology

The posting action in the Manage Carbon Collections app has been renamed from Post Carbon Collection to simply Post. The related posting statuses are:

  • Posting
  • Posted
  • Posting Failed
New Origin Business System Filter

A new Origin Business System filter is now available in the Manage Journal Entries, Display Line Items, and Display Account Balances apps. Selecting an origin business system automatically limits the available master data to values from that system. Likewise, when a master data object that is linked to a specific business system, such as a company code, is selected, the corresponding business system is identified automatically and applied to the remaining filters.

 

Additional Information

If you want to dive deeper into SAP Green Ledger, the SAP Help Portal is a good place to start. It provides detailed information on concepts, configuration, and application usage. You can also check the What’s New section, which is updated with every release and gives a complete overview of the latest additions. To see what's coming next, the SAP Road Map Explorer offers insight into planned features and upcoming innovations. It is updated regularly and outlines how the solution will continue to evolve.

entry.title: Why Your ERP Matters More in the Age of Agentic AI, Not Less
entry.content:
Agentic AI elevates the role of ERP. But only if the architecture can support what comes next.

Every enterprise running SAP has built something valuable: decades of process logic, business rules, transactional history, and institutional knowledge encoded into a system that runs the most critical operations in the company.

Now, as AI reshapes every layer of the enterprise, a question is emerging: does the ERP still matter as much, or does intelligence shift elsewhere?

The answer is unambiguous. In the age of agentic AI, AI does not merely recommend but acts. Your ERP matters more than it ever has. It holds the process context, the data semantics, and the governance infrastructure that agentic AI needs to operate reliably. Without it, AI can suggest. With it, AI can execute.

But here is the critical point: the ERP can only serve as the foundation for agentic AI if its architecture is designed for it. The investment you have made over decades is the accelerator. The question is whether the architecture enables what comes next.

ERP Is Where the Consequential Decisions Happen 

The highest-value AI in your enterprise will not draft emails or summarize meetings. It will operate where the most consequential decisions are made: financial close, supply chain optimization, procurement execution, workforce planning.

These processes live in ERP. They have always lived in ERP. And in an AI-driven enterprise, they do not move somewhere else; they become intelligent where they are.

For AI to act and execute reliably in these processes, it needs three things:

Process context. An AI agent managing your financial close must understand the full lifecycle: what triggered the process, what dependencies exist, what exceptions have occurred historically, what the downstream implications of each action are. This intelligence lives in ERP and nowhere else.

Institutional knowledge. Every enterprise has decades of business-specific decisions encoded in its systems: how exceptions are handled, which approvals are required under which conditions, what tolerances apply in which markets. Without this context, AI operates on generic assumptions, which is unacceptable for core operations.

Governance infrastructure. When AI acts autonomously within your financial reconciliation or procurement workflow, it must operate within clear boundaries: audit trails, authorization controls, compliance guardrails. This is the difference between AI the business trusts and AI the auditor rejects.

No other system holds all three. ERP is where agentic AI must live.

Core Processes Require Process-Native AI 

Consider what happens when an AI agent posts a journal entry that is off by a fraction of a cent. In isolation, trivial. Across thousands of transactions in a regulated financial close, it creates reconciliation failures, audit findings, and restatement risk. 

Or consider procurement: an AI agent that recommends a supplier based on a "best guess" rather than contractual terms, compliance status, and real-time inventory creates liability, not value. 

These are not edge cases. They represent the fundamental standard that core business processes demand: deterministic accuracy. Financial reconciliation must balance to the penny. Supply chain commitments must reflect actual constraints. Procurement decisions must stand up to audit. 

The AI built into SAP is designed for this standard. It delivers deterministic, auditable outcomes grounded in real transactional data, constrained by actual business rules, and validated against the process logic that governs your operations. It does not guess at what your data means; it knows, because it operates within the system that created and maintains that data. 

This is an architectural distinction, not a marketing one. Process-native AI understands the relationships between your transactional tables, the dependencies between process steps, and the governance rules that constrain what is permissible. That structural understanding is what makes the difference between AI that can advise and AI that can act. 

What Makes AI from SAP Different in Core Processes

The question enterprises are asking is clear: what can AI from SAP do within my core processes that other AI cannot?

It knows how your business connects. SAP's Business AI Platform encodes fifty years of enterprise process intelligence: how procure-to-pay, order-to-cash, and design-to-operate actually work at scale. When an AI agent operates within SAP, it inherits the structural understanding of how your processes interconnect. A third-party model can query your data (data is always yours). It cannot replicate this understanding of process interdependency.

Your data stays where it belongs. Enterprises with mature security postures do not expose core transactional data to external AI models. AI provided by SAP operates natively on your business data within your security boundary. There is no extraction, no external processing, no exposure of your most sensitive operational information to models you do not control.

Governance and auditability are built into the platform by design. AI Agent Hub's formal verification workflow creates an approval and ownership record for each agent, providing the audit trail needed for regulatory governance. It also logs agent sessions and captures execution traces, recording what an agent did, which tools it called, and what outcome it produced, with observability and governance capabilities being delivered in phases, intended to support audit and compliance requirements built in from the start, not added after the fact.

It reasons across the full enterprise. No single third-party AI vendor spans procure-to-pay, design-to-operate, and order-to-cash. SAP's cross-functional coverage enables AI that reasons across the enterprise, not within silos. An AI agent optimizing your supply chain understands the financial implications. An agent managing procurement sees the downstream impact on production planning.

It works with the rest of your AI landscape. AI from SAP supports the A2A (Agent-to-Agent) protocol, enabling seamless coordination between SAP agents and third-party AI across your ecosystem. This is not a walled garden: it is a governed platform that interoperates with your broader technology landscape while maintaining the deterministic accuracy and audit-grade governance your core processes require.

 

Why Modernization Unlocks What Comes Next

This is where the modernization imperative becomes concrete.

Enterprises running ECC or on-premises S/4HANA have built a strong operational foundation, one that has run core business processes reliably for decades. Agentic AI introduces a new set of requirements on top of that foundation: real-time semantic interpretation, end-to-end process visibility, and governance frameworks built for autonomous action.

Many customers are already extending their environment with third-party AI tools to start capturing value now. That approach delivers real, if narrow, value. Because these tools sit outside the ERP's native process governance, they operate with less full process context, less audit-grade governance, and less reach across end-to-end process chains than natively embedded AI can provide.

On-premises ERP architecture was designed for a different era. It served that era well. Agentic AI asks something new of the architecture:

  • Real-time semantic data that AI can interpret without transformation
  • End-to-end process visibility without blind spots at system boundaries
  • Native governance frameworks designed for autonomous agents
  • A platform that evolves as AI evolves, with new capabilities on a predictable cadence

These aren't shortcomings in what you've built. They're the next requirements your investment is positioned to meet. Layering tools on top of an on-premises system extends what it can do today. Modernizing the architecture is what determines what it can become.

The Question Is Not Whether Your ERP Matters 

It matters more than ever. 

In the age of agentic AI, ERP becomes the platform where AI acts on your most consequential business decisions, with full context, full governance, and deterministic accuracy. No other system holds the process depth, institutional knowledge, and governance infrastructure that agentic AI requires. 

The question is whether your architecture enables that role. 

Your decades of SAP investment position you to capitalize on what comes next. The process knowledge is the fuel. The business understanding is the differentiator. Modernization is what transforms that investment into the AI-ready foundation your enterprise needs, where governed, process-native intelligence operates across the decisions that matter most. 

And the journey itself has changed. SAP now applies the same AI-first approach to the modernization process: an agent-led toolchain with seven purpose-built migration and modernization assistants that assess your landscape, map custom code, accelerate testing, and reduce overall effort by up to 35%. The path to an AI-ready ERP is now AI-accelerated. 

The gap widens every quarter. New AI agents are released on Cloud ERP, compounding advantage for organizations on modern foundations. The question is not whether to modernize. It is how quickly you want agentic AI working for you. 

To support your ERP modernization journeys, we at SAP have developed the migration and modernization assistants and agents that take over resource and time intensive tasks to accelerate time to value.

Join us for the upcoming webinar where we talk about this  → https://event.on24.com/wcc/r/5365924/0A3E9159F7A3A127BFAA24CE52BE1084/6794304

entry.title: NR4 Canada Report in SAP DRC – Available for On-Premise and Private Edition, soon in Public Edition
entry.content:
Introduction

Canadian tax compliance just got a significant upgrade in SAP. We are excited to announce that the NR4 withholding tax report for Canada is now available in SAP S/4HANA 2023 and SAP S/4HANA 2025, released via SAP Note 3748353 and SAP Note 3780347. In addition, it is planned to be delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS02 (October 2026) and in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608.2 (October 2026).

The report is delivered as part of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) — the same solution that already hosts the T4A-NR report — giving Canadian customers a consistent, unified experience for non-resident withholding tax reporting.

This new report directly addresses a long-standing need expressed by our Canadian customers and complements the existing T4A-NR report that was already available in SAP.

 

What Is the NR4 Report?

The NR4 – Statement of Amounts Paid or Credited to Non-Residents of Canada is a form mandated by the Canada Revenue Agency (CRA). It must be filed by Canadian payers who have paid or credited amounts — such as dividends, royalties, rental income, management fees, or other passive income — to non-residents of Canada during a calendar year.

For every non-resident recipient, a corresponding NR4 slip must be issued, and an NR4 summary must be submitted to the CRA. This is a critical reporting obligation under the Income Tax Act of Canada and is distinct from the T4A-NR, which covers payments to non-residents for services rendered in Canada (such as acting or performance fees).

 

What's New: NR4 Report in SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

With the delivery of this new functionality, SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud now supports the automated generation of NR4 slips and summary directly from the withholding tax postings recorded in the system.

Key capabilities of the new NR4 report include:

  • Extraction of withholding tax data posted against non-resident vendors and business partners
  • Income type classification according to CRA-defined NR4 income codes (e.g., dividends, royalties, rents, management fees)
  • Currency conversion handling for amounts paid in foreign currencies, in line with CRA requirements
  • Country of residence derivation from business partner master data
  • Tax treaty code mapping based on the non-resident's country of tax residency
  • Gross income and withholding tax amounts per recipient, per income type
  • Output in the format required for CRA electronic filing

 

How It Complements the T4A-NR

We have heard your feedback. Many of our Canadian customers highlighted that while the T4A-NR report covered payments to non-residents for services, there was no equivalent SAP-delivered solution for the NR4, which covers passive income payments to non-residents. The NR4 report fills this gap.

Feature

T4A-NR

NR4

Payment type

Services rendered in Canada

Passive income (dividends, royalties, rents, etc.)

Recipient

Non-residents providing services

Non-residents receiving passive income

CRA form

T4A-NR slip

NR4 slip

Available in SAP DRC

Yes

Now available

 

Configuration: What You Need to Set Up

The NR4 report relies on two layers of configuration: the Business Partner master data and the Extended Withholding Tax system settings. Both must be correctly maintained for the report to identify non-resident payees and extract the right data.

At the Business Partner Level

For each non-resident payee, the following must be maintained in the Business Partner master data:

  • Country of residence / tax country — The country where the non-resident is tax-resident. This drives both the reporting logic and the applicable treaty withholding tax rate.
  • Withholding tax type and code — The NR4-relevant withholding tax type and code must be assigned to the business partner, along with the non-resident's tax identification number (Social Insurance Number, Business Number, or foreign TIN, as applicable). The Subject to Withholding Tax flag must also be active.

For full details, refer to: Business Partner Configuration for Withholding Tax – SAP Help Portal

Withholding Tax System Configuration

At the system level, the Extended Withholding Tax framework must be set up in Customizing (SPRO → Financial Accounting Global Settings → Withholding Tax). The key steps are:

  • Check Withholding Tax Countries/Regions — Verify that Canada (CA) is active and correctly configured for withholding tax processing.
  • Define Withholding Tax Keys — Create keys to classify NR4-relevant income categories (e.g. interest, dividends, rents, royalties).
  • Define Withholding Tax Types — Configure types for invoice-based or payment-based posting, depending on when the tax obligation arises.
  • Define Withholding Tax Codes — Set up codes for standard statutory NR4 rates, treaty-reduced rates, and any exempt categories. Rates and formulas are maintained at this level.
  • Define Income Types — CRA income type codes are configured in Customizing to classify the type of income paid to non-residents (e.g. Code 09 for rental income, Code 11 for royalties, Code 13 for management fees, Code 25 for dividends). Each income type is linked to the applicable withholding tax type/code combination, so the correct income classification is applied automatically when that type/code is used in a posting.
  • Assign Withholding Tax Types to Company Codes — Assign the NR4 types to the relevant Canadian company codes so they are active for postings.
  • Activate Extended Withholding Tax — Extended WHT must be activated at company code level; this is a mandatory prerequisite for any withholding posting and reporting.
  • Determine G/L Accounts for Withholding Tax — Assign the appropriate liability accounts (e.g. Non-resident withholding tax payable) for automatic account determination during posting.

For full details, refer to: Withholding Tax Configuration – SAP Help Portal

In SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

The NR4 report is executed from within SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, the same way you already run the T4A-NR report. It allows you to:

  1. Select the reporting year and company code(s)
  2. Filter by withholding tax type relevant to NR4
  3. Generate the output for review, correction, and submission to the CRA

For the full functional documentation of the report, refer to the SAP Help Portal: NR4 Withholding Tax Report for Canada – SAP Help Portal

 

Availability Summary

 

EditionReleaseAvailability
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2023Available – SAP Note 3748353 & 3780347
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2025Available – SAP Note 3748353 & 3780347
SAP S/4HANA On-Premise & SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Private Edition2025 FPS02Planned – October 2026
SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition2608.2Planned – October 2026 

 

Getting Started

If you are on SAP S/4HANA 2023 or 2025 on-premise, implement SAP Note 3748353 & SAP Note 3780347 to activate the NR4 report. Ensure your Basis team has applied the note and that your Finance/Tax team reviews the business partner configuration prerequisites before the next reporting cycle.

If you are on SAP S/4HANA Cloud, the functionality will be available with the 2608.2 release in October 2026 — no manual note implementation required.

 

We Want Your Feedback

The NR4 report was developed in direct response to customer demand, and your input continues to shape what we build next. If you have questions, configuration challenges, or suggestions for future enhancements, we invite you to share your feedback through our dedicated Work Zone community:

Canada ERP Localization – Work Zone

We look forward to hearing from you.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (005) - ABAPシステムのSSL証明書の更新
entry.content:
本記事では、RISE側でのABAPシステムSSL証明書の更新依頼についてご説明させていただきます。
証明書更新に着手される前に、今回登場する証明書関連用語について認識を統一させていただければ幸いです。

証明書関連の用語整理

SSL証明書の更新を進める上で理解しておくべき主要な構成要素について定義いたします。

秘密鍵 (Private Key)暗号化の根幹をなし、厳重な管理が求められる鍵です。
CSR (Certificate Signing Request)秘密鍵を用いて生成されます。これは、認証局（CA）に対して証明書の発行を申請するための「請願書」にあたります。
サーバ証明書 (Signed Certificate)CAがCSRに対して署名を行い、正式に発行された証明書本体です。
中間証明書 (Intermediate Certificate)Root CAとサーバ証明書をつなぎ合わせる橋渡し的な役割を果たします。
Root 証明書 (Root Certificate)CA自身が自己署名した、信頼の基盤となる証明書です。

 

SSL証明書の更新依頼について

SSL証明書の更新は、下記の専用SRテンプレートを通じてご依頼いただけます。
ご利用推奨SRテンプレート: Manage SSL Certificates for SAP Applications and HANA DB

この専用SRテンプレートを用いることで、ABAPシステムだけでなく、Web DispatcherやSCC（SAP Cloud Connector）、HANA DBなどを含む広範囲の証明書更新案件に対応可能です。
現状、SR上でご選択いただける主なオプションは以下の5通りです。


1.新規証明書の作成 (Create Certificate)

SRの情報に基づき、RISE側でCSRを生成し、お客様へ提供いたします。その後、お客様がCAに申請を行い、発行された各種証明書（サーバ、Root、中間）をRISE側にご提示いただくことで、証明書更新作業が依頼できます。

ご注意点:
RISE側にて秘密鍵を保有しているため、PFXファイル作成などで秘密鍵が必要となる場合は、必ずSRにてRISE側の担当者へご依頼いただくか、RISE側の担当者にメールでご連絡をお願いいたします。

通常の作業フロー:

  1. お客様にて本オプションでSRを起票し、CSR作成に必要な情報をご提供ください。
  2. RISEにてCSRを作成し、お客様へ提供いたします。（秘密鍵はRISE側で保有しております）
  3. お客様にてCSRをCAに提出し、証明書発行をご依頼ください。
  4. CAから発行された各種証明書をRISE側にご提供ください。
  5. 当社にてSSL証明書更新作業を実施いたします。
  • ワイルドカード証明書をご利用の場合：
    開発、検証、本番環境などで同一のワイルドカード証明書をご利用の場合、CSR作成およびCAへの発行依頼は開発環境のみで実施すれば問題ございません。検証・本番環境では、開発環境で作成した証明書群（Root, 中間, サーバ証明書および秘密鍵）を利用します。
  • シングルドメイン証明書をご利用の場合：
    環境ごとにCSR作成とCAへの発行依頼を実施していただく必要がございます。

2.証明書の有効期限延長 (Extend Certificate Validity)

オプション1(Create Certificate)と手続きはほぼ同様ですが、既存のPSEファイルからCSRが生成される点が異なります。現在運用中のSSL証明書を延長する場合は、本オプションをご利用いただけます。

3.署名済み証明書によるリニューアル (Renew with Attached Certificate)

お客様側ですでにPFXファイル（pkcs#12）をお持ちの場合に最適なオプションです。

  • CA側にCSR作成およびPFXファイルの提供までご依頼いただける場合は、CA側にて対応をお願いいたします。
  • CA側の対応が難しい場合は、お客様側でCSRを作成し、発行済みの証明書を受領後にPFXファイルとしてまとめることも可能です。

本オプションのメリット:
このフローでは、基本的にSR上のやり取りが簡潔で済み、お客様側のSR起票をもって手続きが完了します。

PFXファイル（pkcs#12）について
PFXファイルは、複数の証明書関連ファイルをパスワード付きの形で一つにまとめたものと考えてください。RISE側へご提供いただく際は、以下のファイルを含めていただくことを推奨いたします。

  1. Root証明書
  2. 中間証明書 (必須ではありませんが、CAから提供された場合はご同封ください)
  3. サーバ証明書
  4. 秘密鍵

また、PFXファイルのパスワードは「Customer Remote Logon Depot」に記載をお願いいたします。詳細については、下記のSAP Notesをご参照ください。
https://me.sap.com/notes/1773689

4.参照システムからのコピー (Copy from Reference System)

こちらはワイルドカード証明書をご利用のお客様向けのオプションです。
開発・検証・本番環境で同一のワイルドカード証明書をご利用の場合、先に開発環境での更新作業が完了した後、検証環境や本番環境の証明書更新を依頼する際に、開発環境を参照システムとして利用していただくことになります。

現在ご利用中の証明書がワイルドカード型か、シングルドメイン型かをご確認いただければ幸いです。

証明書の種類によるカバー範囲の違い

証明書種類カバー範囲例
シングルドメイン1つのFQDNのみをカバーします。www.example.com
ワイルドカード同一ドメイン内の全てのサブドメインをカバーします。*.example.com

 

5.証明書リストへの追加（トラスト登録） (Add Certificate to Certificate List)

特定のPSEの証明書トラストリストに証明書を追加したい場合に利用するオプションです。お客様の業務要件に応じて、SAPSSLS/SAPSSLC/SAPSSLAなどの証明書リストへ追加作業を行います。
下記でよくご利用されるシナリオをご紹介しますが、多くの場合、オプション3（Renew with Attached Certificate）をご利用いただくことで最もシンプルに依頼が可能です。

下記で良く利用するシナリオをご紹介します。なお、シナリオ1とシナリオ2は、オプション3(Renew with Attached Certificate)を利用して下記のようにシンプルに依頼する事が可能です。

Task Required: Renew with Attached Certificate
Certificate Name: SAPSSLS
Upload Certificate: CAから受領、または作成されたPFXファイルをアップロードしてください。
Confirm credentials maintained in Customer Remote Logon Depot if certificate is password protected: Yes

シナリオ1 - ワイルドカード証明書の更新

 下記は、S/4HANA(開発/検証/本番)のABAPシステムのSAPSSLSの証明書更新を例にしております。
基本的な流れは下記となります。

No.対象環境作業担当者　　作業内容必要なファイル　　　補足
1開発環境　　お客様　オプション2を利用してSRを作成‐ 
2 RISECSRを作成し、お客様に提供CSRファイル　　秘密鍵はRISE側で保有している
3 お客様CSRを利用してCAへ証明書の署名を依頼Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書　　		 
4 お客様No3でCAから受領したファイルをRISE側に提供Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書		 
5 RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
6 お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 
7検証環境お客様オプション4を利用してSRを作成‐ 
8 RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
9 お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 
10本番環境お客様オプション4を利用してSRを作成‐ 
11 RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
12 お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 

No.1でのSR作成時のサンプルとなります。

 


開発環境でのSSL証明書の更新作業が完了し、お客様側での動作確認が完了後、オプション4を利用して検証環境(No.7)と本番環境(No.10)でのSSL証明書更新を依頼してください。

Task Required: Renew with Attached Certificate
Certificate Name: SAPSSLS
Reference System: 開発環境ABAPシステムのSIDを指定してください。

シナリオ2 - シングルドメイン証明書の更新

シングルドメイン証明書の場合、開発/検証/本番環境ごとに下記のように実施する必要がございます。

No.作業担当者　作業内容 必要なファイル　　補足
1お客様　オプション2を利用してSRを作成‐ 
2RISECSRを作成し、お客様に提供CSRファイル　　秘密鍵はRISE側で保有している
3お客様CSRを利用してCAへ証明書の署名を依頼Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書		 
4お客様No3でCAから受領したファイルをRISE側に提供Root証明書
中間証明書
サーバ証明書		 
5RISE証明書の更新作業を実施‐ 
6お客様証明書更新作業結果を確認し、問題なければSRのステータスをCloseに変更する‐ 

 

シナリオ3 - Certificate Listへの証明書追加

お客様側の業務要件に応じて、SAPSSLS/SAPSSLCなどへ証明書追加が必要な場合、ご利用願います。

Task Required: Add Certificate to Certificate List
Certificate Name: お客様の業務要件に応じて、SAPSSLS、SAPSSLCなどを指定してください。複数指定可能です。
Upload Certificate: Certificate Listへ追加したい証明書をアップロードしてください。

まとめ

ご利用の証明書の種類を確認し、SSL証明書更新作業を計画していただけますようにお願いします。
また、SSL証明書の更新に多くのオプションが用意されておりますが、オプション3(Renew with Attached Certificate)が最もシンプルとなります。

entry.title: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation
entry.content:
This is the first blog post in a series introducing the topic of AI in SAP Activate. Follow this blog as we will update it with links to other posts as they are released.

Last update: July 15th 2026, with links to additional related blog posts at the end of the article.

In this blog post, I want to share how SAP Activate is evolving with SAP Business AI and what is already available to you today to leverage in your implementation projects. As SAP expands the availability of AI capabilities and more assistants and agents become available for our customers, SAP Activate is keeping pace to ensure you have the guidance you need to adopt, extend, and apply these capabilities effectively during your journey to the Autonomous Suite.

When we think about Business AI in the context of SAP Activate, we organize it into three interconnected dimensions. Each dimension addresses a different aspect of how AI applies in the implementation lifecycle. In the rest of this post, I will walk you through each of these dimensions, explain what is available today, and highlight how you can start benefiting from these capabilities right away.

Three Dimensions of Business AI in SAP Activate

Before I get into the details, let me introduce the framework. The three dimensions are:

  1. Adopting Business AI capabilities and SAP Joule by SAP customers during their implementation and beyond.
  2. Extending SAP solutions with AI capabilities and agents built on the SAP Business AI Platform.
  3. Leveraging AI capabilities for implementation activities.

These dimensions are interconnected but address different audiences and project stages. Let me expand on each one.

1. Adopting SAP Business AI and SAP Joule

The first dimension is about guiding you, the customer, to activate, set up, and adopt SAP Joule and SAP Business AI capabilities as part of your implementation. In other words, this is about integrating AI into how your organization runs its business processes in the SAP Autonomous Suite.

Think of it this way: when your project team starts scoping the implementation and later runs fit-to-standard workshops, they should be considering not only the standard business processes but also the AI capabilities that come with them. Which processes benefit from AI-powered automation or recommendations? Where does SAP Joule provide value to your end users in their day-to-day work? What agents and assistants would enrich the business processes in our organization? These are the questions SAP Activate helps you answer.

Already in 2025, SAP Activate was enhanced with guidance to help customers activate, set up, and adopt SAP Joule and available SAP Business AI capabilities (see the Business AI catalog on SAP Discovery Center). The guidance in SAP Activate includes self-enablement tasks, guidance for running fit-to-standard workshops with AI considerations, and procedures for setting up the necessary integrations. As an example, the information for the setup of SAP Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition is available here (the tasks are tagged with Joule tag).

How do you find this content? You can select the SAP Business AI and SAP Joule tags in the road map viewer in SAP for Me to identify the relevant tasks for your implementation scenario. The flow is straightforward: identify the available SAP Business AI capabilities and SAP Joule features, assess and validate them during fit-to-standard, activate and set up during Realize, and enable your business users to adopt the new capability.

Example of a task for the initial setup of Joule in SAP Cloud ERP Private

We plan to continue expanding this coverage throughout 2026, with increased focus on the new AI capabilities and agents as they become available to customers, and to incorporate steps to consider and embed these capabilities into the scope of your implementation.

Dimension 2: Extending with SAP Business AI Platform Services

The second dimension is for organizations that need to go beyond the pre-built AI capabilities. It supports customers in leveraging the SAP Business AI Platform to build custom AI solutions and agents that extend standard SAP application capabilities in line with the clean core strategy.

This is an important distinction: the first dimension is about adopting what SAP delivers out of the box. The second dimension is about building your own AI-powered extensions on the platform when your business requires something unique. The key principle here is that these extensions follow the clean core approach, ensuring your solution remains upgradeable and maintainable.

SAP Activate for SAP Business Technology Platform already covers various AI-related topics, including guidance for application development on SAP BTP and implementation guidance for AI capabilities. The use of platform services to extend solution guidance is also embedded in the implementation roadmaps for our ERP solutions in the Extensibility work stream, for both private and public cloud solutions. You can access it here.

The flow in your project follows the extensibility work stream: identify and confirm extensibility requirements during fit-to-standard workshops (or earlier if the requirements are already known), leverage SAP Business AI Platform services to extend the standard solution and test during the Realize phase, and finally enable your business users to adopt the new capability.

We plan to continue evolving this implementation guidance as new platform capabilities and services become available, including the new agents and assistants announced at SAPPHIRE 2026.

Dimension 3: Leveraging AI Capabilities for Implementation

The third dimension is where I expect many of you reading this post will find the most immediate value. This is about applying AI capabilities throughout your implementation project to accelerate requirements gathering, design, development, testing, and knowledge transfer across the project lifecycle.

This dimension covers a broad range of AI capabilities like Joule on road map viewer, SAP Joule for Consultants, and embedded AI capabilities in the integrated toolchain, including SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and WalkMe.

AI-Assisted Tasks

Earlier in June, SAP Activate released the first wave of AI-assisted implementation tasks. This initial release included 9 updated SAP Activate tasks, 13 new tested prompts, and updated accelerators, focused on SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

These AI-assisted tasks are designed to support your project team in leveraging AI capabilities during implementation. Rather than focusing on information retrieval, we focused on high-impact tasks and deliverables where AI can make a real difference. SAP Activate delivers tested prompts and example outputs, and in some cases, updated accelerators.

The initial focus is on Joule for Consultants, SAP Cloud ALM, and SAP Test Automation Tool through all project stages, covering seven front-runner topics:

  1. Self-enablement on Joule for Consultants – helping your team get started with the capability.
  2. Project execution and artifacts preparation – accelerating the creation of project management deliverables.
  3. Preparation for fit-to-standard workshops – using AI to help consultants prepare effectively for these critical sessions.
  4. Generating requirements from fit-to-standard workshop artifacts – processing workshop results into requirements and user stories in SAP Cloud ALM with assistance from Joule for Consultants.
  5. Generating design documents for extensions – using AI to draft extension design documentation.
  6. Preparing for data loads  – leveraging AI to help with data migration strategy and approach.
  7. Testing – preparing testing strategy and approach, as well as automating preparation of test scripts, determination of test scope, and management of testing.

An AI-assisted task includes two key elements: an AI-assisted procedure section in the task description that explains how to use the AI capability, and a link to the prompt library. The prompt library provides access to the prompt text, example output, and additional details for prompting the AI tool. We built a centralized prompt library to maintain the integrity of prompts linked to SAP Activate and to ensure all users can access them, even if they do not yet have direct access to the AI capability where a dedicated prompt library may exist.

You can find these tasks by filtering for the AI-Assisted Procedure tag in the SAP Activate roadmap viewer or SAP Cloud ALM when accessing the Task Management app. The SAP Activate team will continue to expand the coverage with AI-assisted tasks in the future. Let us know your feedback on the current tasks and your suggestions for areas to enhance.

Joule in SAP Activate Road Map Viewer on SAP for Me

I also want to highlight a capability that has been available since June 2025 and continues to bring great value to our users. SAP Joule is embedded directly in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me. This is a conversational AI assistant that enables you to surface the guidance you need through natural-language questions, eliminating the need to manually navigate the roadmap structures.

You can ask Joule your SAP Activate-related questions, carry out a conversation, and copy the answers to share with your partners or colleagues. For example, you might ask about the sequence of activities for data migration, or about which accelerators are available for a specific topic. Joule will provide a clear response based on the SAP Activate content.

To try it, navigate to the SAP Activate road map viewer and click on the Joule icon in the top right corner. You can read more about this capability in the blog post from Chandan: Say Hi to Joule in SAP Activate road map viewer.

Outlook

Looking ahead, we will continue enriching SAP Activate with AI guidance for implementation experts – project managers, consultants, developers, and architects – on how to use available AI capabilities during projects.

In the mid-to-long term, we will evolve SAP Activate as new agents and assistants become available in SAP Cloud ALM, aligned with the agentic AI transformation platform vision shared at SAPPHIRE 2026. We anticipate significant evolution as new agents are built and integrated into the offerings through 2026.

I encourage you to explore the AI-assisted tasks in the road map viewer, try Joule on SAP for Me, and share your experience. Please use the comments to this blog post to continue the conversation or reach out to us at sapactivatemethodology@sap.com with your feedback and suggestions. We are excited to hear how you are leveraging these capabilities in your projects.

Other blogs in this series

To deep-dive into AI-assisted tasks and capabilities, access the blogs linked below (we will add the links as the blogs get published).

  • Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for Your Implementation Project by Chandan Gurav
  • AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud by Kiran Bose
  • Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream by Christina Galbreath
  • SAP Activate, AI and Data Management by Daniel Ciecko
  • Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures by Jair Azevedo

Useful Resources

  • SAP Activate road map viewer
  • Introducing Joule
  • Setting Up Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscapes
  • Introducing SAP Joule for Consultants
  • Implementing Joule Across Your Organizational System Landscape

entry.title: SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades: Continuing the Journey from Preparation to Execution
entry.content:
The SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrade Success Series continues this summer with two new sessions focused on how customers can use SAP Joule for Consultants and SAP Cloud ALM to teams move faster and work more consistently across the upgrade lifecycle.

Whether you're preparing for an upcoming upgrade or looking to make upgrade more structured and easier to follow across teams, these sessions provide practical guidance and demonstrations that can be applied directly to your own upgrade projects.  

Register Now

Whether you're joining the series for the first time or continuing from Part 1, the registration page provides access to previous session recordings and registration for the upcoming webinars.

Building on the Foundation

In June, we kicked off the SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrade Success Series with sessions covering:

  • How to plan your SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrade
  • Improving upgrade readiness with SAP Readiness Check
  • Streamlining upgrade planning and execution with Release Upgrade Management  

Together, these sessions focused on helping customers establish a practical starting point for planning and preparing upgrade projects. If you missed Part 1 catch the replays here.

Part 2 of the Upgrade Success Series is designed to help customers build on the foundations established in Part 1 and explore additional tools and approaches that can help improve planning and strengthen execution across the upgrade lifecycle.

 

July 29: SAP Joule for Consultants: Your AI Companion for SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades

Upgrade projects involve many planning, analysis, and coordination activities, and teams need clear guidance to keep the work moving.

This session introduces SAP Joule for Consultants and demonstrates how it can help accelerate and strengthen activities across the upgrade lifecycle.  

What You'll Learn

  • Use SAP Joule for Consultants Across Upgrade Activities – See how it provides guidance and assistance across key upgrade activities, helping teams navigate complex upgrade projects.
  • Build a Stronger Business Case for Upgrades – Learn how the solution helps identify innovations across SAP releases and supports ROI-focused discussions with business stakeholders.
  • Establish Consistent Upgrade Practices – Explore how it can support structured, repeatable upgrade routines to improve planning, execution, and governance.
  • Support Technical Readiness Assessments – See how the solution can assist with Simplification Item analysis, Compatibility Scope checks, add-on evaluation, and custom code analysis.
  • Experience SAP Joule for Consultants in Action – Review real-world upgrade scenarios and live demonstrations showcasing how it supports customers and consultants throughout the upgrade journey.

August 5: SAP Cloud ALM in Action: Managing SAP Cloud ERP Private Upgrades

The second session explores how SAP Cloud ALM can support customers throughout their upgrade journey and serve as a central solution for managing SAP Cloud ERP Private upgrade activities.  

What You'll Learn

  • Manage the End-to-End Upgrade Journey – See how Cloud ALM supports every phase of your upgrade, covering Clean Core, Project Management, and Test Management.
  • Accelerate Upgrade Planning and Execution – Leverage SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Upgrades with SAP Cloud ALM for streamlined project and requirements management.
  • Achieve and Maintain Clean Core Compliance – Use the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard to assess custom code and plan effective remediation aligned with SAP’s Clean Core principles.
  • Manage Regression Testing Effectively – Explore SAP Cloud ALM Test Management capabilities to plan, execute, and monitor regression testing activities during upgrades.
  • Support Post-Upgrade Success – Leverage monitoring capabilities to validate system performance and operational readiness after go-live.
  • Explore What's Next – Gain insight into planned innovations on the SAP Cloud ALM roadmap designed to further support future upgrade projects.

Ready for Part 2?

The next phase of the Upgrade Success Series explores how SAP Joule for Consultants and SAP Cloud ALM can support Cloud ERP Private upgrade projects.

If you missed Part 1, revisit our previous blog for an overview of the sessions and key topics covered.

Access the recordings and register for upcoming sessions:

Register Now

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud で外部拡張アプリから呼び出すカスタム CDS ビューを作ってみた — 標準 API にないフィールドが必要なときの選択肢 —
entry.content:
1. はじめに

SAP S/4HANA の仕訳データを外部システムから取得したい場合、まず候補に挙がるのが標準 OData API「A_JournalEntryItemBasic」（OP_API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV_0001）です。仕訳明細の基本情報を取得できる使いやすい API ですが、すべてのフィールドをカバーしているわけではありません。

参照: SAP Business Accelerator Hub - Journal Entry Item - Read (Basic): https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV_0001/overview

今回のユースケースは「反対仕訳されていない債務伝票だけを外部アプリから取り出したい」というものです。SAP の Manage Journal Entries アプリでは「Is Reversed」（反対仕訳済み）と「Is Reversing」（反対仕訳伝票）の列で状態を確認できます。しかし、標準 API A_JournalEntryItemBasic にはこれらのフィールドが含まれていないため、そのままでは条件絞り込みができません。

図: Manage Journal Entries アプリ — Is Reversed / Is Reversing 列で反対仕訳の状態を確認できる

 

この課題を解決するため、本記事では SAP の内部 CDS ビュー「I_OperationalAcctgDocCube」をデータソースとして、キーユーザー拡張アプリ「Custom CDS Views」でカスタム CDS ビューを作成し、OData サービスとして外部公開する手順を解説します。ABAP 開発ツール（ADT/Eclipse）は不要で、ブラウザ上の操作だけで完結します。

 

2. 標準 API の限界と、使いたい CDS ビュー

2.1 標準 API A_JournalEntryItemBasic の概要

A_JournalEntryItemBasic（OP_API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV_0001）は、仕訳明細を取得する代表的な OData V2 API です。会社コード・勘定科目・転記日付・金額など多くのフィールドを提供しています。

ただし、以下のフィールドは含まれていません：

  • IsReversed：この伝票が反対仕訳済みかどうか
  • IsReversal：この伝票自体が反対仕訳伝票かどうか

そのため、このAPIだけでは「反対仕訳されていない伝票のみ」という絞り込みができません。

 

2.2 I_OperationalAcctgDocCube とは

I_OperationalAcctgDocCube（正式名: Operational Journal Entry Item - Cube）は、SAP が提供する内部の CDS ビューです。仕訳データを広範にカバーしており、IsReversed / IsReversal フィールドも含まれています。

フィールドの例）

フィールド名

型

説明

IsReversed

CHAR(1)

この伝票が反対仕訳済みかどうか

IsReversal

CHAR(1)

この伝票自体が反対仕訳伝票かどうか

CompanyCode

CHAR(4)

会社コード

AccountingDocument

CHAR(10)

仕訳伝票番号

FiscalYear

NUMC(4)

会計年度

AccountingDocumentItem

NUMC(3)

明細番号

 

ただし、このビューは外部（OData 等）から直接アクセスすることはできません。そこで、このビューをデータソースとするカスタム CDS ビューを作成し、外部公開します。

参照: SAP Help Portal — I_OperationalAcctgDocCube
https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/ee6ff9b281d8448f96b4fe6c89f2bdc8/54b2fdbc55dc46ea82afd64b6f2f17bf.html

 

3. アーキテクチャ概要

今回構築する仕組みの全体像は以下のとおりです：

外部アプリ / SAP BTP  →  OData サービス（ZZ1_BLOG_JOURNALENTRY_2_CDS）  →  カスタム CDS ビュー（ZZ1_Blog_JournalEntry_2）  →  I_OperationalAcctgDocCube

キーユーザー拡張アプリ「Custom CDS Views」でカスタムビューを作成・Publish した後、SAP GUI のトランザクション /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE でゲートウェイに登録することで、OData V2 エンドポイントとして外部から呼び出せるようになります。

 

4. カスタム CDS ビューの作成（キーユーザー拡張アプリ）

4.1 前提条件と起動

Custom CDS Views アプリを使用するには以下が必要です：

  • キーユーザー拡張機能がシステムで有効化されていること
  • Custom CDS Views アプリへのアクセス権限（ロール）が付与されていること
  • 例: SAP_BR_ANALYTICS_SPECIALIST、SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC など（システムによって異なる）

Fiori Launchpad の「Extensibility」タブから「Custom CDS Views」タイルをクリックして起動します。

図: Fiori Launchpad — Extensibility タブから Custom CDS Views を選択

 

4.2 新規ビューの作成

Custom CDS Views の一覧画面で「Create」ボタンをクリックすると、作成ダイアログが表示されます。以下のように入力します。

項目

設定値

備考

Label

Blog_JournalEntry_2

任意の説明ラベル

Name

ZZ1_Blog_JournalEntry_2

自動補完される（ZZ1_ プレフィックス）

Scenario

External API

OData として外部公開する場合はこれを選択

 

図: Custom CDS Views 一覧 — Create ボタン

 

図: 新規作成ダイアログ — Label / Name / Scenario を入力して Create

 

Scenario に「External API」を選択することで、このビューが OData サービスとして外部公開される設定になります。

 

4.3 データソースの追加（Data Sources タブ）

Create 後に遷移する Custom CDS View Details 画面で、「Data Sources」 タブが選択されています。「Add」→「Primary Data Source」をクリックし、データソース選択ダイアログを開きます。

検索ボックスに「I_OperationalAcctgDocCube」と入力して検索します。

Name

Label

Status

I_OperationalAcctgDocCube

Operational Journal Entry Item - Cube

Released

 

「I_OperationalAcctgDocCube」を選択して確定します。

図: Data Sources タブ — Add → Primary Data Source を選択

 

図: I_OperationalAcctgDocCube を検索して選択

 

4.4 フィールドの追加（Elements タブ）

「Elements」タブに移動すると、キーフィールド（CompanyCode, FiscalYear, AccountingDocument, AccountingDocumentItem）が自動で追加されています。「Add」→「Elements」をクリックしてフィールド選択ダイアログを開きます。

検索ボックスに「isRev」と入力すると、以下のフィールドが表示されます。両方にチェックを入れて「OK」をクリックします。

Alias

Type

Label

IsReversal

CHAR(1)

Is Reversing

IsReversed

CHAR(1)

Is Reversed

 

図: Elements タブ — Add → Elements を選択

 

図: フィールド追加後の Elements 一覧（IsReversal, IsReversed が追加された状態）

 

図: 「isRev」で検索し IsReversal / IsReversed を両方チェック

 

4.5 チェックとパブリッシュ

フィールドの追加が完了したら、画面下部の「Check」ボタンをクリックして整合性チェックを実行します。右上に「Check Successful」と表示されれば問題ありません。

続けて「Publish」ボタンをクリックします。ビューがアクティベートされ、OData サービスが生成されます。右上に「Publishing Successful」と表示され、Status が「Published」に変わったことを確認します。

図: Check ボタン実行後 — 右上に「Check Successful」の表示

 

図: Publish ボタンを押す前の確認

 

図: Publishing Successful — Status: Published に変わった状態

 

 

5. OData サービスのゲートウェイへの登録（/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE）

Custom CDS Views アプリで Publish しただけでは、OData サービスはバックエンドに生成されているものの、SAP Gateway（フロントエンドシステム）にはまだ登録されていません。外部からアクセスするには、トランザクション /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE でゲートウェイへの登録が別途必要です。

 

5.1 トランザクション MAINT_SERVICE の起動

SAP GUI でトランザクション /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE（Activate and Maintain Services）を起動し、「Add Service」ボタンをクリックします。

図: /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE — Activate and Maintain Services 画面

 

5.2 サービスの検索と追加

Add Selected Services 画面で以下を入力して「Get Services」を実行します。

項目

値

System Alias

バックエンドのシステムエイリアス（例: S4H_PGW。システムによって異なる）

Technical Service Name

ZZ1*（ワイルドカードで検索）

検索結果に「ZZ1_BLOG_JOURNALENTRY_2_CDS」（Description: Blog_JournalEntry_2）が表示されます。行を選択して「Add Selected Services」をクリックします。

図: ZZ1* で検索し ZZ1_BLOG_JOURNALENTRY_2_CDS を選択して追加

 

5.3 パッケージの割り当てと完了

Add Service ダイアログが表示されます。Package Assignment でパッケージを指定します。

選択肢

説明

用途

$TMP（Local Object）

トランスポート不要のローカルオブジェクト

検証・テスト目的のみ

カスタムパッケージ（例: ZCUSTOM 等）

トランスポートリクエストに乗せて管理

本番運用・他環境への移送が必要な場合

 

Custom CDS Views アプリで Publish した際にも Transport Request が発行されます。/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE でのパッケージ指定はそのトランスポートと合わせるのが自然です。適切なパッケージ名はシステム管理者・Basis 担当者に確認してください。

図: Add Service ダイアログ — パッケージ割り当て（$TMP または適切なパッケージを指定）

 

確定すると「Service 'ZZ1_BLOG_JOURNALENTRY_2_CDS' was created and its metadata was loaded successfully.」というメッセージが表示されます。サービス一覧に戻り、登録されたサービスと System Aliases の表示を確認します。

図: 登録完了 — サービス一覧と System Aliases の確認

 

6. OData サービスの確認とテスト

6.1 SAP Gateway Client での動作確認

/IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE の画面下部「ICF Nodes」から「SAP Gateway Client」ボタンをクリックして Gateway Client を起動します（または /IWFND/SUTIL_GW_CLIENT を直接実行）。

図: MAINT_SERVICE の ICF Nodes から SAP Gateway Client を起動

 

まずトップレベルエンドポイントを呼び出して、エンティティセットが正しく登録されていることを確認します。

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZZ1_BLOG_JOURNALENTRY_2_CDS/?$format=xml

HTTP 200 が返り、レスポンスに ZZ1_Blog_JournalEntry_2 のコレクションが表示されれば成功です。

図: トップレベル呼び出し — HTTP 200 と ZZ1_Blog_JournalEntry_2 コレクションの確認

 

6.2 フィルタクエリの例

反対仕訳されていない伝票のみを取得するには、以下のクエリを使います。

GET /sap/opu/odata/sap/ZZ1_BLOG_JOURNALENTRY_2_CDS/ZZ1_Blog_JournalEntry_2?$filter=IsReversed eq false and IsReversal eq false

 

HTTP 200 が返り、レスポンス XML の各エントリに以下が含まれているもののみであれば、正しく動作しています。

<d:IsReversal>false</d:IsReversal>
<d:IsReversed>false</d:IsReversed>

 

図: $filter=IsReversed eq false and IsReversal eq false — HTTP 200 でフィルタ済みデータが返る

 

7. 外部システムからの利用（応用例）

7.1 SAP BTP / SAP Integration Suite での利用

SAP BTP と SAP S/4HANA を接続し、HTTP Destination を登録することで、SAP Integration Suite などから OData V2 接続でこのサービスを呼び出せます。未反転の債務伝票一覧を定期取得してレポートや通知フローを構築するといった活用が考えられます。

7.2 カスタムアプリ（UI5 等）での利用

SAPUI5 アプリでは OData モデルバインディングで一覧表示が可能です。IsReversed / IsReversal の値をアイコンや色で可視化するなど、業務に即した画面を構築できます。

 

8. 注意点・Tips

  • I_OperationalAcctgDocCube は Cube ビューのため、パフォーマンスに注意が必要です。大量データ取得時は $top / $skip やフィルタを必ず指定しましょう。
  • /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE でのゲートウェイ登録は Publish 後に別途必要です（Custom CDS Views アプリだけでは外部から呼べない）。
  • System Alias の設定はシステム構成によって異なる。Basis 担当者に確認しましょう。
  • 公開するフィールドは必要最小限に絞り、機密性の高いフィールドを誤って公開しないよう注意しましょう。

 

9. まとめ

本記事では、標準 API A_JournalEntryItemBasic では取得できない IsReversed / IsReversal フィールドを外部から利用するために、キーユーザー拡張アプリ「Custom CDS Views」でカスタム CDS ビューを作成・外部公開する手順を解説しました。

手順をまとめると以下のとおりです。

  1. Fiori Launchpad の Extensibility タブから Custom CDS Views アプリを起動する
  2. 新規ビューを作成し、Scenario に「External API」を選択する
  3. Data Sources タブで I_OperationalAcctgDocCube を Primary Data Source として追加する
  4. Elements タブで IsReversal / IsReversed を追加する
  5. Check → Publish でビューをアクティベートする
  6. /IWFND/MAINT_SERVICE でゲートウェイにサービスを登録する
  7. SAP Gateway Client で $filter=IsReversed eq false and IsReversal eq false を使って動作確認する

 

ABAP 開発ツールなしにブラウザ操作だけで完結し、かつアップグレード保護された形で拡張できる点が、SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud におけるキーユーザー拡張の大きなメリットです。標準 API で充足できない要件に直面したとき、カスタム CDS ビューの活用を検討してみてください。

 

参考リンク

  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub - Journal Entry Item - Read (Basic): https://api.sap.com/api/OP_API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV_0001/overview
  • SAP Help Portal - I_OperationalAcctgDocCube:
    https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/ee6ff9b281d8448f96b4fe6c89f2bdc8/54b2fdbc55dc46ea82afd64b6f2f17bf.html

entry.title: Clean Core Levels A–D: how to classify your custom ABAP — and what to do with each grade
entry.content:
Disclosure: I'm the author of this post and the creator of Clean-Core.io, a free, non-commercial community tool I mention once near the end. This post is educational; the tool is optional.

Overview

With the 2025 update to the ABAP extensibility guide, SAP evolved the familiar 3-tier extensibility model into the clean core level concept — a four-grade classification of custom extensions: A, B, C, D. Instead of a blunt "clean vs. not clean", it tells you how closely each extension aligns with Clean Core, and it maps directly to ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) behaviour:

 

Level Usage of SAP objects in custom development ATC behaviour

AReleased SAP APIs (local & remote) and extension pointsNo message
BClassic SAP APIs and extension points (SAP-recommended)Priority 3 · info
CInternal SAP APIs (conditionally clean via the changelog-for-SAP-objects check)Priority 2 · warning
DNot-recommended objects & extension technologies (modifications, implicit enhancements)Priority 1 · error

Level A is the cleanest — on-stack ABAP Cloud or side-by-side on SAP BTP. Levels B–D describe classic ABAP, from SAP-recommended (B) down to not-recommended (D).

Why this matters now

Every custom object you carry into S/4HANA has to be defensible. The move from a binary "clean/not clean" to A–D is a gift — it lets you transform at your own pace, prioritising the worst first instead of trying to reach "100% clean" overnight.

The real problem

Knowing the model is easy. Grading thousands of custom objects against it is not. ATC flags readiness object-by-object, and the Cloudification Repository tells you which standard objects are released — but turning that into a per-object A/B/C/D verdict plus a remediation path, across a grown Z-landscape, is still mostly manual and inconsistent between teams.

A practical classification flow

  1. Identify the custom object and what it touches (tables, APIs, function modules, enhancements).
  2. Look up each dependency: released API/extension point → A; classic-but-official API → B; internal SAP object → C; modification / not-recommended access → D.
  3. Decide remediation per grade: A keep · B keep, plan to lift later · C verify via the changelog-for-SAP-objects approach and wrap behind a released interface · D replace before the upgrade.

A worked example

A classic Z_CUSTOMER_REPORT:

SELECT kunnr, name1 FROM kna1 INTO TABLE @lt_cust.   " direct read of an internal standard table

Classify: direct access to KNA1 (an internal SAP object, not a released API) → Level C (ATC: warning). A direct write would be Level D (ATC: error).

Remediate: map to the released CDS view I_Customer → the same logic becomes Level A (ATC: no message):

SELECT Customer, CustomerName FROM I_Customer INTO TABLE @lt_cust.
