One dependency, one grade, one clear next step. Repeat across the landscape and you get a prioritised backlog: fix D first (upgrade blockers), lift C, schedule B, keep A.

How I approached tooling this (optional)

To avoid doing this by hand, I built Clean-Core.io — a free, non-commercial community tool. It runs a deterministic evidence engine first (parsing the code, mapping dependencies against the catalog), then AI on top, and derives an A–D grade per detected object — aligned to the clean core level concept and its ATC priorities. It is complementary to ATC/ADT, not a replacement, and every output is a draft for a qualified architect to review. Free: clean-core.io (link in the comments).

Conclusion

The move from 3-tier to A–D isn't a relabel — it's a better mental model for prioritising custom-code remediation. Give each dependency an explicit grade, start with your Level D objects, and turn "we have a lot of Z-code" into a defensible, upgrade-ready plan.

How are you classifying your custom code against the new A–D levels today — by hand, ATC, or something else? I'd genuinely like to compare approaches in the comments.

entry.title: How SAP MDG lets you download and fix problem records directly from your process
entry.content:
Introduction

If you have ever run a mass change or consolidation process in SAP Master Data Governance, you know how valuable the validation step is. However, when the validation step stops due to errors, the erroneous records are stuck inside the system. Error messages are visible, but there was no way to extract, fix, and re-upload them.

And when a file upload fails due to invalid source IDs, all you get was a generic message like "Load contains invalid source IDs: 2". No details, no way to tell which records are actually affected. Either way, users end up rebuilding files manually.

This pain point was also one of the most strongly requested topics in SAP Customer Influence, with many customers highlighting the need for a smoother way to handle failed records. This was addressed, both following the same principle: giving a possibility to download a file of the problematic records, in exactly the format needed to fix and re-upload.

Feature 1: Export Process Data with Messages in the Validation Step

The Problem

In a consolidation or mass change process, the validation step checks the record correctness. When errors are found, the process stops and shows aggregated KPIs (e.g., "47 records with errors"). You can drill into the Details UI to see which records failed and why, but there was no way to export that subset for offline correction and re-upload.

What Was Delivered

The details UI of the validation step directly includes a new export action. When pressing the button it navigates to the export app and the filter for selected records is automatically set.

What it does:

  • Exports all records currently displayed in the validation step's overview tab
  • Includes the related messages (errors, warnings) as an additional sheet/section in the exported file
  • Uses the standard MDG export file format, the exported file can be edited offline and re-uploaded directly into a new import or mass processing run

After downloading the file errors and warnings are shown in the ! Messages ! tab of your excel sheet:

Availability

  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2022
  • SAP S/4HANA cloud public edition

Feature 2: Download Invalid Records During Upload

The Problem

When uploading a file for a mass process, the system performs a technical validation, e.g. it checks whether the IDs in the file exist in the system. If they do not, it raises a generic error:

"Load contains invalid source IDs: 3"

That is all the users got, just generic information, no more details. Which three records? Which IDs? Users had to manually inspect the file, compare it with the system, and figure it out themselves.

What Was Delivered

Whenever invalid records in a file are detected, it is possible now to export those records from the original file by an “Export”-button in File Import Log area.

The file is in the same format as the upload, so users can correct the records (fix or remove the invalid IDs) and re-upload without rebuilding the file from scratch.

Availability

  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 in classic mode
  • MDG on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 in cloud-ready mode
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Why These Two Features Matter Together

Both features address the same need: when something goes wrong in an MDG process, users need a way to act quickly. With both features, you can download the problematic records as a file, fix them offline, and use that file as the starting point for a follow-up process.

Feature 1 helps when errors are found during the validation step. Feature 2 helps even earlier, when the file upload itself fails due to invalid IDs. In both cases, the result is the same: a downloadable file with exactly the records that need to be fixed. This means users no longer must rebuild files from scratch or investigate manually which records caused the error. This saves time, reduces the load on technical teams, and makes the whole process more reliable, especially when working with large data volumes where even a small error rate can mean hundreds of records to fix.

Have you ever used one of these features?

_________________

Further Links and Resources

  • SAP Master Data Governance community https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/master-data-governance
  • SAP Help Portal https://help.sap.com/docs/MASTER_DATA_GOVERNANCE - Various help documents for e.g. MDG on S/4HANA Cloud Private and Cloud Public Edition
  • SAP Influence Portal https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#campaign/3497 - SAP Master Data Governance on SAP S/4HANA: Submit new ideas or vote on existing requests to shape the MDG roadmap
  • SAP Learning https://learning.sap.com/courses/sap-master-data-governance-on-sap-s-4hana - Learning content covering MDG strategy, domains and processes related to MDG central governance, consolidation and mass processing end-to-end

entry.title: Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For additional information, visit the main blog here. 

Project Managers are expected to balance strategic planning with day-to-day execution while keeping projects on schedule, within budget, and aligned with business objectives. To help streamline these activities, the SAP Activate road map viewer now includes AI-assisted procedures for tasks within the Project Management workstream.

These AI-Assisted procedures are designed to help project managers quickly create high-quality project deliverables by combining SAP Activate best practices with generative AI. Rather than starting from a blank page, project teams can leverage guided AI prompts to accelerate planning, improve consistency, and reduce manual effort.

What Are AI-Assisted Procedures?

AI-Assisted procedures provide step-by-step guidance for using generative AI to complete common project management activities. Each procedure includes a structured prompt that incorporates SAP Activate methodology and best practices, helping generate a tailored starting point that can be reviewed and refined for your specific project.

The generated content is intended to support project teams by providing a solid first draft while allowing project managers to apply their expertise and project-specific knowledge before finalizing the deliverable.

The following Project Management tasks now include AI-Assisted procedures with the SAP Activate road map viewer:

Develop a Project Charter

A well-defined project charter establishes the project’s purpose, objectives, scope, governance, and success criteria. The AI-Assisted procedure helps generate a comprehensive project charter by incorporating information such as project goals, stakeholders, timelines, assumptions, constraints, risks, and governance structure.

This provides project managers with a structured draft that can be customized to meet the organization's specific requirements.

Estimate Backlog Items

Accurate backlog estimation is essential for planning releases and managing project delivery. The AI-Assisted procedure assists teams in developing consistent effort estimates by analyzing backlog item descriptions and applying estimation guidance based on SAP Activate best practices.

The resulting estimates can be reviewed and refined by the project team during backlog and sprint planning activities.

Develop a Hypercare Support Plan

Preparing for a post-go-live support is critical to ensuring a successful transition into operations. The AI-Assisted procedure helps generate a structured Hypercare Support plan that outlines support objectives, roles and responsibilities, issue management processes, communication plans, monitoring activities, escalation paths, and exit criteria.

This provides implementation teams with a comprehensive starting point for planning the Hypercare period.

Handover Operations Responsibility

A smooth transition from the implementation project to the operational support organization is key to long-term success. The AI-Assisted procedure assists project teams in developing an operational handover plan that defines transition activities, knowledge transfer, documentation requirements, support responsibilities, and operational readiness.

The generated plan helps ensure that operational teams receive the information they need to effectively support the solution after go-live.

Communicate Project Status and Progress

Keeping stakeholders informed with timely, accurate, and consistent project updates is essential for effective project governance. The AI-Assisted procedure helps project managers create a comprehensive project status report by generating a structured summary of overall project health, milestone progress, schedule and budget status, key accomplishments, risks and issues, upcoming activities, and decisions requiring stakeholder attention.

This provides project managers with a professional, SAP Activate-aligned starting point that can be reviewed, tailored with project specific information, and shared with stakeholders to support informed decision making and transparent project communication.

Why Use AI-Assisted Procedures?

By incorporating AI-Assisted procedures into the SAP Activate methodology project teams can:

  • Accelerate the creation of common project management deliverables.
  • Start with structured, SAP Activate-aligned content instead of a blank document.
  • Improve consistency across projects by leveraging standardized guidance.
  • Reduce the time spent drafting project documentation.
  • Allow project managers to focus more on project leadership, stakeholder engagement, and decision-making.

These procedures are designed to enhance, not replace, the expertise of experienced project managers. Human review remains an essential part of the process to validate accuracy, tailor the content to the project, and ensure alignment with organizational standards.

The AI-Assisted Procedure tag helps you quickly identify tasks that include guidance for using generative AI. These tasks contain an AI-Assisted procedure and a link to the SAP Activate Prompt Library, making it easy to generate first drafts of project deliverables while following SAP Activate best practices.

entry.title: AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series that explains how to leverage SAP Business AI in your implementation projects. For additional context, please refer to the main blog post, SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation

Clean Core and Extensibility in SAP Activate

SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private includes a dedicated Clean Core Success Plan that spans all project phases. The methodology establishes clean core governance early. A Solution Standardization Board is set up during Prepare, extensibility scenarios are evaluated and classified before detailed design begins, and Clean Core Quality Gates run through the project to track progress against clean core objectives.

For extensibility, SAP's clean core approach distinguishes between two architectural patterns. On-stack extensibility covers extensions that run directly on SAP S/4HANA and require tight coupling with core data, transactions, or applications. Side-by-side extensibility on SAP BTP covers loosely coupled extensions that interact with the core through released remote APIs and events. When planning extensions, SAP recommends starting with what SAP BTP can offer, as this supports upgrade readiness and innovation potential. Where business or technical requirements clearly call for proximity to the core, on-stack extensions remain the right choice.

On-stack extensions should be built using the ABAP Cloud development model, which enforces clean core compliance through compiler checks and restricts usage to publicly released SAP APIs and extension points. Classic extensibility (custom ABAP development using SAP internal objects or modifications) is still available in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and on-premise, but carries higher upgrade risk and sits at the lower end of the clean core level scale. The goal is to reach Level A extensions built with SAP Build using only publicly released, stable interfaces, and to eliminate Level D extensions that use non-recommended objects, modifications, or direct write access to SAP tables.

The extensibility workstream in the Explore phase is where most of the documentation work happens: consolidating requirements from fit-to-standard workshops, producing functional specifications for each RICEFW object, and then moving into detailed technical design for those that need custom development. These are necessary steps, but they are time-consuming.

AI-Assisted Procedures in SAP Activate

SAP Activate now includes AI-Assisted Procedures for selected tasks across the SAP Cloud ERP Private - New Implementation roadmap. Each one is embedded directly in the task description in the SAP Roadmap Viewer and connects to a specific prompt in the SAP Activate Prompt Library, which is hosted within the Roadmap Viewer.

Tasks with an AI-Assisted Procedure are tagged in the SAP Roadmap Viewer with two tags: SAP Business AI and AI-Assisted Procedure. These tags can be used to filter the roadmap and quickly identify all tasks that carry AI-assisted guidance, without navigating through the full task list.

For the extensibility workstream, three of these AI-Assisted Procedures support the documentation and design effort leading up to development.

 

SAP Joule for Consultants

SAP Joule for Consultants is the SAP Business AI capability used to execute the prompts discussed in this blog. It is the SAP Consulting capability within Joule, available to consultants working on SAP engagements, that provides access to expert templates and project-specific prompt execution. Consultants provide their project inputs, attach relevant documents where required, and SAP Joule for Consultants generates a structured first-draft deliverable ready for review and refinement.

The AI-Assisted Procedures in this blog all target SAP Joule for Consultants. SAP Activate is expanding the scope of SAP Business AI across the methodology, and future procedures will leverage additional Joule and SAP Business AI capabilities beyond the consulting capability described here. For more information, see the SAP Joule for Consultants Service Guide.

Using the SAP Activate Prompt Library

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is accessible directly from the SAP Roadmap Viewer. Each prompt entry is structured to give consultants everything they need to run the prompt and understand the expected output before they start. Each entry includes the following fields:

Field Description

Prompt NameThe name of the prompt, which corresponds to the deliverable it produces.
RoadmapThe SAP Activate roadmap or roadmaps the prompt applies to.
TaskThe specific task in the roadmap where the prompt is used.
Target JouleThe Joule capability used to execute the prompt.
Expert TemplateAn optional expert template that structures the Joule session for the specific use case.
Attachments / RequirementsThe documents required or recommended as input when running the prompt.
Sample OutputA worked example showing the format and depth of the deliverable the prompt produces.

The prompt library is the starting point before running any AI-Assisted Procedure. Reviewing the sample output for the relevant prompt gives consultants a clear picture of what SAP Joule for Consultants will produce and what inputs are needed.

 

Extensibility Design and Planning Prompts within SAP Activate

Document Extensibility Requirements

The "Document Extensibility Requirements" task in the Explore phase is where all extensibility needs identified during fit-to-standard workshops get formally documented, classified, and signed off by the Solution Standardization Board before design begins. Producing a complete Functional Specification for each RICEFW object (covering the gap justification, process flows, authorization requirements, data model considerations, and test conditions) is the core output of this task and typically requires significant consultant time per object.

SAP Activate Task: Document Extensibility Requirements
SAP Joule for Consultants Prompt: Functional Specification Document

Takes the RICEFW object list from the workshops as its primary input, with the SAP Activate Lean Specifications Template as an optional attachment. Produces a complete, structured FSD for each object covering gap analysis, functional description, authorization matrix, process flows, data model, dependencies, and test scenarios in a format directly usable in MS Word.

Detail Design of Extension with Classic Extensibility Techniques

Once requirements are documented and clean-core alternatives are confirmed as insufficient, the detailed technical design for each classic extension needs to be produced. This requires evaluating the extension against SAP's Clean Core Extensibility framework and producing a specification precise enough for development: entry points, APIs or extension technologies, enhancement logic, data model changes, and authorizations.

SAP Activate Task: Detail Design of Extension with Classic Extensibility Techniques
SAP Joule for Consultants Prompt: Design Analysis and Technical Specification

Takes the Functional Specification document from the previous task as its input. For each RICEFW object, it determines the clean core compliance level with justification, then generates the full technical design specification: entry point, released API or extension technology, enhancement logic and process flow, data model considerations, and authorization requirements. Where a cleaner alternative exists or should be evaluated in a future release, the output flags it.

These two prompts connect directly. The FSD produced by the first is the mandatory input for the second. The RICEFW object list from workshops flows through to a complete technical design specification with one human review step in between.

Development of Clean Core Compliant Extensions

Before development begins, all extension objects need consistent, project-specific technical names that comply with the agreed customer namespace, Clean Core tier, and SAP naming rules. Without this, naming decisions get made ad hoc by individual developers, leading to inconsistencies across workstreams that are difficult to correct once objects exist in the system.

SAP Activate Task: Development of Clean Core Compliant Extensions
SAP Joule for Consultants Prompt: Extensibility Naming Conventions

Takes the project RICEFW List, a sample Functional Specification Document header, and the project Extensibility Blueprint as inputs. Produces a complete, project-ready Extensibility Naming Convention document covering all three Clean Core extensibility tiers: Developer, Key User, and Side-by-Side, with naming patterns, length constraints, examples, and counter-examples for every object type. Includes a Complete Object Naming Register that validates every object from the RICEFW List against the agreed conventions and flags deviations.

All three prompts connect as a chain. The RICEFW object list from workshops flows through to a complete technical design specification and a validated naming convention document, with one human review step between each stage.

Step 1: Document Extensibility Requirements
Functional Specification Document prompt → FSD per RICEFW object

FSD output becomes mandatory input for Step 2

Step 2: Detail Design of Extension with Classic Extensibility Techniques
Design Analysis and Technical Specification prompt → Clean Core assessment + Technical Design Spec

Technical Design Spec and RICEFW List become inputs for Step 3

Step 3: Development of Clean Core Compliant Extensions
Extensibility Naming Conventions prompt → Project-ready Naming Convention document + Complete Object Naming Register

All three prompts are available in the SAP Activate Prompt Library.

What's Next

The SAP Activate team is actively identifying more ways to leverage SAP Business AI within the SAP Activate methodology to accelerate the implementation of SAP solutions. The extensibility prompts are part of a broader effort to embed AI assistance at the points in a project where documentation and decision-making effort is highest. With the addition of the Extensibility Naming Conventions prompt, the AI-assisted extensibility coverage now extends into the Realize phase, supporting naming governance before development begins.

All three prompts are accessible in the SAP Activate Prompt Library within the SAP Roadmap Viewer: Functional Specification Document, Design Analysis and Technical Specification, and Extensibility Naming Conventions.

Related Resources

  • SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer
  • SAP Joule for Consultants Service Guide
  • SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation - Expert Portal
  • Clean Core Extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

entry.title: Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures
entry.content:
This blog post is part of a series highlighting AI capabilities in SAP Activate. For an overview of the complete series, visit the main blog post here: SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions ... - SAP Community

What if AI could help your project team prepare Fit-to-Standard workshops faster, more consistently, and with greater confidence?

Now it can.

Why Fit-to-Standard preparation matters

Preparing for Fit-to-Standard workshops is one of the most important activities in an SAP implementation.

Before the workshops begin, the project team must:

  • Understand and validate the implementation scope.
  • Map the business processes and solutions to be demonstrated.
  • Identify the appropriate stakeholders.
  • Define clear roles and responsibilities.
  • Prepare a detailed workshop agenda.
  • Determine the required inputs and expected outcomes.

This preparation is essential—but it can also require significant time and coordination.

What if an AI-assisted procedure could help the project team perform these activities?

We have exciting news.

AI assistance is now embedded in SAP Activate

SAP Activate now includes AI-Assisted Procedures for selected tasks within the Application: Design and Configuration workstream of the SAP Cloud ERP Private – New Implementation roadmap.

These procedures combine SAP Activate best practices with generative AI to help project teams create comprehensive Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation and planning materials more efficiently.

Each AI-Assisted Procedure is:

  • Embedded directly in the task description in SAP Roadmap Viewer.
  • Connected to a task-specific prompt.
  • Supported by the SAP Activate Prompt Library.
  • Designed to fit naturally into the implementation workflow.

This means project teams can access AI guidance precisely where they need it—without leaving SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Let’s explore “Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops”

Open the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops task in SAP Roadmap Viewer.

Inside the task, you will find an AI-Assisted Procedure that guides you through the workshop preparation process. Open the procedure and follow steps 1 through 9.

The procedure helps you organize the necessary project information and use the corresponding prompt to generate a structured Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation plan.

Two new accelerators to support the process

We have also created two accelerators to help project teams use the procedure and its prompt more effectively:

  1. Fit-to-Standard Workshop Notes — a structured template for capturing the information required to prepare and run the prompt.
  2. SAP Activate Prompt Library — a collection of prompts supporting multiple activities across the SAP Activate roadmaps, including the Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops prompt.

The SAP Activate Prompt Library is available directly from the SAP Roadmap Viewer. Simply click the prompt referenced in the task to access it.

Each prompt entry provides consultants with the information they need to:

  • Understand the purpose of the prompt.
  • Identify the required inputs.
  • Execute the prompt correctly.
  • Review the expected output before getting started.

A structured prompt based on the COP Framework

The prompts follow the COP Prompt Framework, which is organized around:

  • Context — the project background and information the AI needs.
  • Objectives — the results the prompt should produce.
  • Preferences — the expected structure, level of detail, and output format.

This framework makes the prompts easier to understand, execute, and adapt to the specific needs of each project.

What does the prompt generate?

The Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation Plan prompt can generate:

  1. Scope Validation Checklist — a pre-workshop sign-off checklist for the System Integrator Lead and Customer Project Manager.
  2. Business Process Scoping Matrix — maps in-scope functional areas to SAP Best Practices scope item IDs, workshop dates, and business owners.
  3. Business Stakeholder Pre-Work — an actionable checklist of items due at least five business days before each workshop.
  4. System Readiness Checklist — covers sandbox provisioning, SAP Best Practices activation, master data, user access, and SAP Cloud ALM setup.
  5. Workshop Agenda Templates — includes a general full-day Fit-to-Standard agenda and tailored versions for functional areas such as Finance, Procure-to-Pay, Order-to-Cash, Manufacturing, Warehouse Management, and Asset Management.
  6. Fit-to-Standard Tracking Template — provides a detailed gap log, including one row for each delta requirement identified in the workshop notes.
  7. Roles and RACI Matrix — defines accountability across key customer, system integrator, architecture, consulting, and project management roles.
  8. Gap Capture Guidance for SAP Cloud ALM — provides a step-by-step process for recording gaps, completing mandatory attributes, assigning resolution categories, and maintaining traceability.
  9. Risks and Mitigation Strategies — creates a risk register covering workshop execution, scope, stakeholders, and technical readiness.
  10. Classification Decision Tree — supports consistent classification of requirements as Fit, Partial Fit, Gap, Out of Scope, or Deferred.

A representative example is also available in the Sample Output section of the prompt library.

What are the benefits?

AI-Assisted Procedures can help project teams:

  • Accelerate Fit-to-Standard workshop preparation.
  • Generate agendas based on the project scope.
  • Improve visibility into required resources, stakeholders, and inputs.
  • Reduce the time needed to validate workshop topics and schedules.
  • Establish a structured timetable for the topics to be discussed.
  • Apply SAP Activate guidance more consistently.
  • Start from a comprehensive, structured plan that the project team can review and refine.

AI supports the preparation—but the project team remains responsible for reviewing, validating, and adapting the output to the project context.

This is just the beginning

AI-Assisted Procedures bring SAP Activate guidance and generative AI together directly within the implementation workflow.

And this is only the beginning—more AI-assisted content is on the way.

Are you ready to use the Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation prompt in your next project?

Try it and share your experience in the comments. We would love to learn how you used the prompt, what value it brought to your project, and which SAP Activate task you would like to see supported next.

entry.title: News from SAP Activate - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2608
entry.content:
We are pleased to announce the updates to SAP Activate, including the availability of AI-Assisted tasks. We’re also excited to share the newly refreshed SAP Activate page on sap.com, which offers a clearer, more intuitive overview of the SAP Activate methodology. 

This release delivers a range of enhancements across SAP Activate assets, strengthening implementation guidance and improving usability. As always, our focus remains on simplifying and optimizing the implementation journey to provide a more seamless experience for our users. 

Below are the key highlights for this release:

  • SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: AI-Assisted Tasks Now Available
  • What’s new in Activate Content Library (ACL)
  • SAP Activate Community page
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private
  • Updates to SAP Activate for Conversion of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system to 3-system landscape
  • Updates to SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2608 Release

SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: AI-Assisted Tasks Now Available

SAP Activate now incorporates SAP Business AI across three dimensions: (1) guiding customers to adopt SAP Joule and Business AI capabilities during implementation, (2) supporting extensions built on the SAP Business AI Platform in line with clean core, and (3) leveraging AI to accelerate implementation activities across the project lifecycle. 

As part of this evolution, the first wave of AI-assisted implementation tasks was released earlier in June and enhanced during July, delivering 18 AI-assisted tasks, 21 new tested prompts, and updated accelerators. The initial focus is on the use of SAP Cloud ALM AI capabilities, SAP Joule for Consultants, and Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

They cover a range of project activities — from self-enablement and project artifact preparation to fit-to-standard workshop support, requirements generation, extension design, data migration planning, and testing. You can identify these tasks using the "AI-Assisted Procedure" tag in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me, where SAP Joule is also available as a conversational assistant to help you navigate implementation guidance. 

Read the full blog post here for a detailed walkthrough of all three dimensions and how to get started. 

SAP Activate Community | SAP Activate Blogs

Join the SAP Activate Community to explore valuable content and resources while connecting with SAP partners, clients, and internal colleagues by participating in discussions and sharing knowledge. Make sure to follow the  SAP Activate tag to access blog posts like those highlighted here, along with additional insights shared by SAP Activate experts within the community. 

Recap of some blog posts from the community:

  • SAP Business AI in SAP Activate: Three Dimensions of AI for Your Implementation by Jan Musil
  • Introducing the SAP Activate Prompt Library: Curated AI Prompts for your Implementation Project by Chandan Gurav
  • AI-Assisted Extensibility Planning & Design in SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Cloud by Kiran Bose
  • SAP Activate, AI and Data Management by Daniel Ciecko
  • Work Smarter with AI-Assisted Tasks in the SAP Activate Project Management Workstream by Christina Galbreath
  • Transform Your Fit-to-Standard Workshop Preparation with AI-Assisted Procedures by Jair Azevedo
  • New SAP Activate Roadmap for SAP Sales Cloud V2 by Chandan Gurav
  • SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors by user alejandroazca
  • Update on SAP Activate Road Map Viewer Accelerators for Central Finance by Carla Nascimento
  • Exploring the New Guided Walk-Thru for SAP Activate for SAP IBP by user MabelleRM
  • Quality Gates: Leveraging the continuous quality check (CQC) Service Report for Project Readiness by Christina Galbreath
  • Foundational News: Implementing SAP Business Data Cloud – New Roadmap Release in SAP Roadmap Viewer by Ariane Hoffman
  • Do you want to boost your SAP project skills with Agile & SAP Activate by Margit Wagner

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP S/HANA Cloud Public Edition in 2608

The SAP Activate for SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are highlights for this release.

  • Expanded AI support: AI-assisted procedures have been added across multiple implementation tasks to help customers and consultants accelerate delivery using Business AI and Joule for Consultants. A new AI-Assisted Procedure tag and Prompt Library make AI-enabled guidance and prompts easier to find.
  • Business AI integration: AI-powered scenarios are now embedded throughout the implementation methodology, providing guidance at the point where it is most relevant.
  • Enhanced Fit-to-Standard guidance: New Fit-to-Standard overview video and RACI matrix help project teams better understand workshop activities and stakeholder responsibilities. See task Prepare for Fit-to-Standard Workshops.
  • Integrated Quality Gates: Quality Gates are now incorporated into the Continuous Quality Check (CQC) process, enabling customers to complete phase-end questionnaires directly in SAP Cloud ALM and submit them to SAP.

Improved upgrade methodology: The upgrade roadmap for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape) has been expanded with more detailed guidance and stronger project management support for major upgrade milestones. SAP Activate for the Upgrade of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (3-system landscape).

Updates of SAP Activate for Cloud ERP Private

SAP Activate, the standardized framework for the RISE with SAP Methodology, has been enhanced with numerous updates, as detailed in the release notes.

This release significantly expands AI capabilities by introducing AI-assisted procedures, prompt-based accelerators, and enhanced SAP Cloud ALM guidance that help consultants streamline project execution, improve documentation quality, and increase consistency using SAP Joule for Consultants. In addition, the release strengthens Clean Core integration guidance with the latest SAP recommendations and introduces new AI-powered learning and change management capabilities to accelerate end-user enablement and content development. 

Key highlights include:

  • Expanded SAP Business AI integration across the SAP Activate for SAP Cloud ERP Private methodology, embedding AI-assisted procedures and new prompt-based accelerators into key project lifecycle tasks — including backlog estimation, cutover planning, hypercare support, and operations handover — enabling consultants to accelerate execution and improve output quality with SAP Joule for Consultants.
  • Enhanced SAP Cloud ALM guidance with AI capabilities, adding new procedure steps and accelerators for document summarization and AI-assisted requirement generation across project setup, workshop execution, and requirements documentation tasks, helping project teams work faster and more consistently.
  • Strengthened Clean Core integration guidance by incorporating SAP Note 3690029 — Integration Technologies and Frameworks in Context of Clean Core Integration — across all PCE scenarios, ensuring consultants have up-to-date direction on compliant integration approaches aligned with clean core principles.

Improved learning and change management enablement by introducing AI-assisted accelerators for user learning and change management, including a new micro-app hub integration via SAP Discovery Center, supporting more targeted and efficient learning needs analysis and content development planning.

See the release notes for a complete list of all improvements. 

Updates of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2-system landscape to 3-system landscape 

We are happy to share the highlights of the latest improvements to the SAP Activate guidance for our customers converting their SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition environments from a 2-system landscape to a 2-system landscape.

These are the key updates:

  • Updated assignment of tasks to the “Day 1 Post-Conversion Checklist” tag to include the tasks added to the Realize phase to address specific post-conversion steps.
  • Multiple updates for test data refresh and test data conversion - revised procedure details in the tasks Prepare for Test Data Conversion Service and Prepare for Test Data Refresh Service. Additionally, refreshed the accelerator Test Data Conversion Checklist.
  • Added new task Copy Workforce Users and Request Test Data Refresh after the Conversion in the Realize phase, with additional details for the customer to copy workforce users and submit a request for TDR, including the addition of new accelerators.
  • Updated task Prepare for Test Data Refresh Service in the Prepare phase with information about how to request the service.
  • Updated task Prepare for Test Data Conversion Service in the Prepare phase with information about how to request the service.

Updates of SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors

The SAP Activate for SAP SuccessFactors roadmap has been enhanced based on product management input, system functionality changes, and customer feedback. Below are the highlights:

  • Integrated Quality Gates: Phase-end Quality Gates are now part of the Continuous Quality Check (CQC) process, allowing questionnaires to be completed and submitted directly through SAP Cloud ALM.
  • Business AI integration: AI-powered scenarios are now embedded throughout the implementation methodology to provide contextual guidance when teams need it.
  • Joule integration: New guidance has been added for configuring and enabling Joule as part of the implementation process. Joule integration.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Test Automation Tool 2608 Release

The CE2608 release introduces additional enhancements to simplify test scoping, improve test data management, and enable easier comparison test statuses across different Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) executions.

Key highlights include:

  • AI-Powered Test Scoping in Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS)
    • Enhanced by combining AI with SAP Standard code-change analysis to automatically recommend the most relevant Post Upgrade Test (PUT) scope.
    • Smarter test scope recommendations: The AI model considers customer usage, process complexity, change-to-touch ratio, historical regression behavior, and SAP standard code changes to identify the optimal regression scope.
    • Reduced testing effort and costs: Customers can execute fewer, more relevant regression tests, reducing validation effort, shortening execution time, and lowering overall testing costs without compromising quality.
    • No additional customer action required: AI-based test scoping is enabled by default for Post Upgrade Tests, while still allowing customers to modify the proposed scope in the Manage Upgrade Test app if needed.
  • Simplifying Test Data Management with the Creation of Multiple Data Variants and Assigning them to TDC Variants
    • New Dataset entity for simplified test data management: A new Dataset feature groups multiple Test Data Container (TDC) variants into a reusable entity, making test data easier to organize and manage.
    • Create multiple data variants in one step: User can create and assign multiple data variants simultaneously, significantly reducing repetitive manual effort and speeding up test plan preparation.
    • Automatic assignment of test data: For test plans with multiple TDCs, selecting a dataset automatically maps the appropriate TDC variants, improving consistency and minimizing errors.
    • Greater efficiency and productivity: The enhancements streamline test data management, reduce manual work, improve organization, and allow testing teams to focus more on validating business processes.
  • Post Upgrade Tests (PUT) Comparator enhancements
    • Compare test executions across releases: The enhanced Post Upgrade Test (PUT) Comparator allows users to compare two completed executions of the same execution variant to quickly identify differences, analyze failures, and validate upgrade readiness.
    • Improved visibility and scalability: Enhanced comparison results and pagination make it easier to review and navigate large test execution datasets with hundreds of matching scope items.
    • Advanced filtering for faster analysis: New filtering capabilities enable users to quickly focus on failed, successful, or impacted scope items, reducing manual review effort.
    • Better usability and navigation: User interface improvements streamline comparison workflows, making Post Upgrade Test analysis faster and more intuitive.

Overall, these enhancements significantly improve testing efficiency, increase data accuracy, reduce manual effort, and boost productivity across post-upgrade test automation workflows. For more details, refer to the blog.

 

Please share your feedback and questions in the comments to this blog post or directly via the feedback function in the SAP Activate road map viewer on SAP for Me.

Run simple – SAP Activate – Accelerate and simplify the adoption of SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

entry.title: Custom Fields with Field Status Group control in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
One of the most significant changes introduced by SAP S/4HANA is the unification of financial tables into a single source of truth: the Universal Journal, or table ACDOCA. With this new table, creating new fields is no longer an exclusive task for ABAP developers. Through SAP Fiori, functional consultants now have access to an application that allows us to create custom fields and assign them to Field Status Groups.

Depending on the intended business use of the field, it can be populated automatically using a BAdI (BADI_FINS_ACDOC_POSTING_EVENTS) or manually during journal entry posting.

In this blog, I will explain through a practical example how to create a custom field available for data entry during General Journal Entries posting. The allowed values for this field will be restricted by a drop-down list (Code List), and its field status requirement (Required/Optional/Hidden) will be controlled by the Field Status Group.

Creating the Custom Field

To create a custom field, we must use the SAP Fiori app Custom Fields (App ID: F1481😞

Upon opening the app, select the "Create" (plus icon) button located in the upper right corner:

A pop-up dialog will appear with the following fields:

Since the new field must be available for data entry when creating a G/L journal entry, you must select FINS_CODING_BLOCK in the Business Context field:

Enter the label for the new field and select the field type from the drop-down list. For this exercise, this custom field should only be populated during posting using specific values, so we select the Code List option. Selecting this option enables new configuration fields within the pop-up to define the drop-down values:

  • Length: Defines the character length of the drop-down values during journal entry creation, which directly determines the technical length of the custom field.
  • List Values: Use this section to manage the dropdown values and their corresponding descriptions.

After filling in all required fields, select the Create button at the bottom right of the pop-up window. To publish the field, navigate to the details page:

Important: Since this field will be controlled via the Field Status Group (Transaction OBC4), it is not necessary to enable user interfaces.

To finalize the field creation process, proceed to publish it by clicking the Publish button at the bottom right of the screen. Once published, the field is successfully created and will exist within table ACDOCA:

Including the Field in the Coding Block

This activity must be performed via SAP GUI, either by navigating through SPRO to the Customizing activity Edit Coding Block or by directly executing transaction code OXK3:

First and foremost, this activity allows the inclusion of custom fields into FI General Ledger accounts, enabling them within the Field Status Groups. It also allows fields to be integrated into MM Stock Management, MM Purchasing, and CO Line Items.

For our example, this process will enable the new custom field on an FI subscreen, making it visible and available during journal entry posting.

Upon accessing this customizing activity, the transaction will display two options. We must select Customer–Defined Account Assignments, and within it, Customer Include Structure. This is where the CI_COBL structure is located, which we will use to link our newly created custom field:

To add the field, double-click on the CI_COBL structure and populate only the Component and Component Type fields using the exact field identifier generated in the Custom Fields SAP Fiori app:

Proceed to save the changes. Then, double-click on the structure once again to activate the new field:

Once the activation process is executed, an activation log will be generated where you can review any occurring errors. Typically, two errors are displayed: one related to table COEP and another related to table MSEG:

To resolve the error associated with table COEP, follow the instructions provided in KBA 2384731 - Correction program: COEP and replacement object V_COEP have different number of columns. This SAP Note will instruct you to execute the report FCO_CDS_VIEW_GENERATE. Once this program has run successfully, the new custom field will be visible in the V_COEP view:

To resolve the error associated with table MSEG, follow the instructions provided in KBA 2242679 - Redirect inconsistency - Proxy Substitution. The assistance of an ABAP developer will be required to fix this error. Since this is a walkthrough example, we can ignore the error in table MSEG and proceed; however, it is critical to always resolve all occurring errors to prevent future data inconsistencies in the production environment.

Creating the Coding Block Subscreen

Once the errors have been resolved and the CI_COBL structure is fully activated, the account assignment subscreen must be created. This configuration activity can be accessed either via transaction code OXK3 or by directly executing transaction OXK1.

Upon executing the transaction, select the "Create" (New) option. The system will prompt you to create a new subscreen number within the allowed custom range of 9000 – 9999:

The new field will be available, and you will only need to assign a position number to it and generate the screen:

The new subscreen will be created under the Customer Subscreens section:

If the new custom field is missing from the subscreen you are creating, follow the recommendations provided in KBA 3609989 - Added Z Coding Block Field Not Available on OXK1 Screen.

Updating the Field Status Group

This is the final activity required to complete the end-to-end setup. The first task that must be performed—not due to a strict process sequence, but because it is commonly overlooked—is to change the field status of the new custom field within the Posting Keys that will be used for the corresponding accounts. This is maintained via transaction OB41 (Posting Keys). For this exercise, the changes were applied only to posting keys 40 (Debit) and 50 (Credit).

The second task is to update the Field Status Variant where the field status group assigned to the target G/L accounts is located. This configuration activity can be accessed via the SPRO reference IMG menu path or by directly executing transaction code OBC4:

Once inside the variant and the specific field status group, you must select the "Additional account assignments" field group:

If the technical name of the new field appears as "Subgroup text is missing", you must follow the resolution steps outlined in KBA 3592675 - OBC4: the short name is "Subgroup text is missing":

Once the steps suggested in KBA 3592675 are completed, the field name will be displayed correctly. You can then proceed to select the required field status (e.g., Optional or Required entry) and save the changes:

Testing with a Journal Entry Document

Once all system preparation and customizing activities are completed, we can post financial documents using the new custom field.

For our example, we will execute the SAP Fiori app Post General Journal Entries (App ID: F0718) and select a G/L account that has the modified field status group assigned within its Master Data. Within the line item details, select the "More" subscreen button, where you will see the new custom field available with its drop-down list:

Conclusion

Throughout this document, we've seen that this approach to managing custom fields is specific to general ledger postings. Using custom fields in other modules might require ABAP support. We've verified that we can manage the creation of these fields from start to finish and even enable them in field status groups. I hope this post is helpful for all functional consultants who enjoy exploring the limits of SAP.

entry.title: How to implement Finance on a UPA System - The Training Portfolio at a Glance!
entry.content:
Ready to build practical SAP S/4HANA finance skills for a world of parallel accounting?

Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) is becoming an important foundation for finance implementations in SAP S/4HANA. If you work with financial accounting, asset accounting, or end-to-end record-to-report processes, now is a great time to strengthen your knowledge and get hands-on with learning content built on a UPA-enabled training system.

Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) helps organizations prepare and present financial statements according to different accounting standards, such as group GAAP and local GAAP, by using ledgers across end-to-end processes. This supports consistent parallel value flows throughout the value chain — from inventory and production to overhead and asset accounting. With UPA, organizations benefit from full audit trails, greater automation and accuracy for parallel legal valuations, and simplified configuration compared with traditional parallel accounting approaches.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, UPA is activated by default. For SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP S/4HANA, it must be activated — ideally at the beginning of a greenfield implementation.

To help learners prepare for this shift, we have been expanding our SAP Learning portfolio with courses that run on a UPA-enabled training system.

The journey started with the launch of the learning journey Implementing Record-to-Report in SAP S/4HANA, which helps learners build practical implementation skills for core record-to-report processes.

We have now added two more courses to support learners who want to deepen their expertise in accounts payable, accounts receivable, and asset accounting:

  • Managing Customer and Vendor Accounts
  • Configuring Basic Settings for Asset Accounting

Explore the new courses today and continue building the skills you need for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition finance implementations. More courses are already in preparation, so stay tuned as the portfolio continues to grow over the coming months.

entry.title: Q2 2026 Innovations: SAP Enterprise Service Management
entry.content:
Q2 went by fast! I hope that you all were able to participate in SAPPHIRE 2026 either in person or virtually. The SAP Autonomous Enterprise was announced and a shout out of huge thanks to our service customers Pfizer and Doehler for sharing your incredible stories & insights about your transformation, AI adoption and value outcomes with SAP Service Cloud and SAP ESM! Our next value-add event is queued up -  SAP Connect 2026 is coming to Las Vegas, October 5-7 and we welcome our customers and partners to secure an early bird rate by August 10th to discover how to maximize value of your solutions, explore best practices in AI and business transformation approaches and much, much more!

Before I jump into our Q2 innovations for SAP ESM there are a couple of exciting opportunities I’d like to share.

  • It’s not too late to join our SAP ESM webcast on Tuesday, July 21st, focused on HR Service Delivery: AI, Data, and Experience: From Transactions to Intelligent HR Services also featuring a widely recognized expert Anita Lettink. If you’re reading post-event, click the link above and watch the replay!
  • Also, there is a new SAP Learning module for SAP ESM: Integrating SAP Enterprise Service Management. This course is available at no cost and is designed specifically for administrators, consultants, and business users to deepen their knowledge of SAP Enterprise Service Management. This course focuses on integration configuration to enable execution of end-to-end scenarios delivering true value and operational efficiency across the organization.

Now back to the focus of this blog – our Q2 innovations for SAP ESM! There are so many innovations and improvements please register for our Q2 Innovation webcast July 29th hosted by our product team.

Self-Service Catalog

The Self-Service Catalog provides employees with a guided, intuitive way to find and request the services they need, without requiring an understanding of the underlying processes or systems. Requests submitted through the appropriate topic catalog or tile are automatically resolved or routed based on the inquiry context, which reduces manual triage effort, frustration in solutioning and shortens resolution times. For service agents, this means fewer incomplete or misrouted cases and more time spent on higher-value work. For employees, it means a simpler, more predictable experience when engaging with their enterprise or shared service teams.

Service Catalog configuration

                                                                                             

Service Catalog Self Service

 

Content Package Framework

The Content Package Framework enables organizations to deploy SAP ESM more quickly by leveraging prebuilt, reusable configurations for case types, workflows, and catalogs. Rather than building service processes from the ground up, customers and SI partners can start from proven templates and adapt them to specific business needs. This reduces implementation effort, simplifies rollout across lines of business, and helps organizations standardize service delivery at scale. Content packages for HR service delivery are planned for a future release.

New Email Editor

The new email editor provides service agents with a modern, streamlined interface for composing, editing, and managing customer communications. Designed with an AI-first architecture in mind, the editor supports faster email creation while maintaining the quality and consistency expected in service interactions. Coupled with the AI email drafter capability, agents truly spend less time formatting and more time focused on resolving customer issues, which improves response times and communication quality.

 

Email editor

Creation of Sales Objects from the Customer Hub

While this capability is more closely aligned with SAP Service Cloud, sales support and B2B supplier support are common use cases we see deployed for SAP ESM as well. With that said, service agents can now create and manage leads, opportunities, appointments, and sales orders directly from the service agent desktop when the interaction calls for it, reducing clicks and upskilling time. The capability supports closer alignment between service and sales teams and delivers that seamless experience you expect between SAP solutions.

 Self-Service Enhancements

Two enhancements provide administrators with greater control over the self-service experience:

  • Edit Form in Self-Service During Case Lifecycle feature allows administrators to define conditions under which a form remains editable, giving employees the ability to update information at appropriate points in the case lifecycle without agent involvement.
  • Adapt Case Details Screen per Case Type feature allows administrators to configure the Case Details screen in Self-Service on a per-case-type basis, ensuring that users only see fields and actions that are relevant to their specific scenario.

Together, these enhancements help drive higher self-service adoption and reduce unnecessary agent handoffs.

Embedded Component for Custom Services

Administrators can now define list views or object forms within a custom service and embed them into custom user interfaces, as well as selected standard UIs. By mapping parameters from the host page, related custom records can be viewed and worked on directly within the relevant UI view. This capability makes it easier to extend SAP ESM to fit unique business processes and ensures that agents and users have access to contextual information exactly where they need it, without navigating away from their current task.

New Self-Service Experience (Restricted Release)

A new Self-Service experience for SAP ESM is being introduced as a restricted release. Aligned with the latest self-service catalog capabilities, the new experience is designed to support cross-functional deployment across the enterprise, providing instant solutions to common inquiries and enabling contextual case creation and management. As this is a restricted release, customers interested in exploring the capability are encouraged to request access via the solution. Early feedback from customers will play an important role in shaping the general availability release, and participation is strongly encouraged.

Sign In as Another User

Administrators can now sign in as another business user seeing the same functionality and authorizations as that user. This allows administrators to reproduce and troubleshoot issues from the user’s perspective without screen sharing or password exchange. This results in faster issue resolution, stronger security practices, and a more efficient support process for both administrators and end users.

Analytics Improvements

This release delivers two important improvements to analytics capabilities:

  • Embedded SAC Widgets allow SAP Analytics Cloud visualizations to be embedded directly into the SAP ESM interface, displaying focused analytics with individual tiles within the daily flow of various business objects such as cases, customers and opportunities. These widgets can also be incorporated into mashups for more comprehensive analytics overall.
  • Custom Service Entities in Analytics allow custom service entities to be used as data sources in SAP Analytics Cloud. Once a custom entity is marked as Analytics Relevant, the system automatically generates a corresponding data source that appears under Custom Data Sources in the Query Designer, where it can be combined with standard data sources to produce tailored analytics content.

These enhancements bring insights closer to where work happens and reduce the need to switch between systems for data-driven decision-making.

Approval Process Simplification

Two enhancements to approval processes help organizations improve consistency and reduce delays.

  • Configure Approval and Rejection Reasons: Administrators can now configure predefined approval and rejection reasons, create custom reason lists, assign them to specific approval processes, and control whether reason selection is mandatory or optional. This supports more consistent decision-making and improves auditability across approval workflows.
  • Automatic Approval Settings: Configuration allowing approval steps to be automatically approved or rejected if no action is taken within a defined duration can be defined from one hour to ten days. This helps ensure that workflows continue to progress even when approvers are unavailable, reducing bottlenecks and shortening overall case cycle times.

Accelerating Case Resolution with UX Simplicity and Automation

Several enhancements in this release focus on simplifying the case experience and giving administrators more configuration control. A few examples include:

  • Case Data Retention Rules by Case Type: This feature allows administrators to configure retention rules for automated transaction depersonalization on a per-case-type basis, helping the organization meet legal, regional, and business-specific data retention requirements.
  • Multiple Catalogs Mapped to One Case Type: The ability to map multiple catalogs to one case type allows administrators to assign additional category catalogs to a case type, minimizing configuration effort where similar case types can be used across various business functions.
  • Hide Category Levels in Case: This feature allows administrators to selectively hide specific category levels within cases, providing agents with a cleaner and more focused interface by removing unnecessary hierarchical information.

Case adaptation

These enhancements help streamline the agent experience while giving administrators the flexibility to align case management with compliance, business, and operational requirements.

Useful Information

For additional Q2 innovations relevant for SAP Enterprise Service Management please reference Kirsi Tarvainen’s blog on SAP Service Cloud and register for the upcoming Q2 Release Webinar. To stay up-to-date as we release capabilities each month and to get a full view of what’s new for Q2, reference the What’s New page in our help portal. Also, reference these valuable links for more on SAP ESM and SAP CX solutions:

SAP CX Q2 2020 Innovation News

SAP Learning ESM Course

SAP Service Cloud V2 Certification Course

SAP Release Navigator

SAP ESM’s free trial Get hands-on experience today with an HR service delivery scenario (Finance shared services coming soon).

Use the SAP Enterprise Service Management managed tag when posting on SAP ESM topics in the community!

And finally, thank you. The continued growth of this community, and the willingness of its members to share, question, and collaborate, is what moves SAP ESM forward. Every SAP employee, partner, and customer benefits from the collective knowledge exchanged here and it’s genuinely appreciated.

entry.title: Batch Management in SAP LGM
entry.content:
Batch Management is used to track and manage materials in batches (lots) that share the same characteristics, such as Shelf life expiration date, Batch status, Country/region of origin, Date of manufacture etc.

This is needed to ensure Traceability, Quality Control, Shelf Life and Expiry Management, Inventory Differentiation, Warehouse and Production operations and also it helps during product recalls and are needed across a variety of Industries like Pharmaceuticals, Food & Beverage, Chemicals, Cosmetics, Medical Devices, Agriculture/Seeds and Manufacturing with lot-controlled materials.

Considering the importance of this feature SAP has introduced it in SAP LGM also. This blog covers an overview of how the functionality is designed as per current development.

We will cover it under following topics

  1. Batch Creation
  2. Stock Upload without Purchase Order
  3. Logistics Processes with Batch Management (Inbound & Outbound)
  4. Exception – How to handle scenario with missing Batch in S/4 document.

Lets start with the first topic

Batch Creation

  1. Batch Creation in S/4 system and transfer to LGM
    • Activate the Product as Batch Relevant and Define Batch in S/4 with key details like Date of Manufacture / SLED etc.

  • Within SAP LGM system using the App - Manage Replication Configuration, create a Replication variant that will be used for replicating batches from S/4 system to LGM

  • Using the App - Schedule Batch Replication configure the job to schedule the replication. This can be based on a schedule or even immediate

  • Once the Batches are replicated, they can be seen using the App - Display Batches

Let's now look at the Stock upload process without the Purchase Order

The Process starts with MIGO based GR posting in S/4 system. This creates Delivery in the S/4 system with batch details.

Based on the set up configurations a Logistical Demand is created and the batch details are available at the item level.

The corresponding Inbound Warehouse request also contains the batch details and once the warehousing process is completed the stock is updated back into S/4 system.

 

 

Let's now look at the Inbound and Outbound process:

Inbound Delivery based Batch Process

Once the Batch has been created in S/4 system and replicated in LGM, create the PO with the Batch Managed Product. The Batch details can either be provided in the PO or in Delivery.

Create Delivery for the PO with batch details.

On saving the Delivery, Logistical Demand is created in SAP LGM and the Batch Details are also seen in the document item.

 

 

The details also flow thru into the Inbound Warehouse request which can then be processed further to complete the warehouse processing.

 

Outbound process with Batch Management

The Outbound process starts with the creation of a Sales order and the Outbound Delivery within S/4 with a Batch managed product and Batch details.

Once the delivery is distributed it creates a Logistical Demand in SAP LGM. The Batch can be seen here at the item level.

In the above scenario this starts the warehouse processes and the Batch details flow into the Outbound Warehouse Request and also the Packing Work Center as can be seen below:

Once the Packing is completed, we can use the Monitor Handling Units app to view the batch details in the Handling Unit.

Once the Warehouse processes are completed the Goods Issue is posted and the stock details for the Batch Managed Product are updated in the S/4 system.

 

Now let's look at the LGM system behavior in the case where we have Missing Batch Details in a Delivery with Batch Managed Product

In this scenario we have a Batch Managed Product but have not provided the batch details in S/4 while creating the delivery. The Delivery is distributed to the S/4 system and is not editable in S/4 system.

Since the product is Batch Managed with missing details, the LGM system validates the data and the Logistical Demand is created with an Error Status as shown below.

To correct the issue, this Logistical Demand can be "Cancelled".

The Cancellation triggers an Integration task to "Decline" the Inbound Delivery in this case which then updates the Delivery in S/4 system.

The system then updates the Good Movement Status to "Completed" with a "Delivered Quantity" as "0".

We can now create a new Inbound Delivery with the required batch details and then transfer the same to LGM correctly with the Batch details.

That's all on the topic of Batch Management in SAP LGM.

Hope this helps.

For More Details on this topic, please refer to the below Links

What's New Viewer - SAP Logistics Management | SAP Help Portal

Batch Management | SAP Help Portal

Batch Replication | SAP Help Portal

Display Batches | SAP Help Portal
